Name: Acid Splash
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You form a sphere of corrosive liquid at a point you choose within range, and it bursts in a 5-foot-radius area. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 Acid damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Aid
Components: V, S, M (a strip of white cloth)
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Select up to three creatures you can see within range. Each one gains 5 additional Hit Points, and its Hit Point maximum increases by the same amount for the duration.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Each target's Hit Points increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Alarm
Components: V, S, M (a bell and silver wire)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin
Text:
You create a ward to detect intruders. Choose a door, window, or an area no larger than a 20-foot cube within range. Until the spell ends, the ward alerts you whenever a creature touches or enters the protected space. When casting the spell, you can exempt any creatures you choose. You also decide whether the alarm is audible or mental.

Audible Alarm. A bell-like sound rings for 10 seconds and can be heard within 60 feet of the ward.

Mental Alarm. You receive a mental alert if you are within 1 mile of the warded area, and this alert wakes you if you are asleep.

Name: Alter Self
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Moon (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer
Text:
You reshape your body in one of several ways. Choose an option below; its effects last for the duration. During the spell, you may use a Magic action to switch to a different option.

Aquatic Adaptation. Gills form on your neck and webbing grows between your fingers. You can breathe underwater and gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

Change Appearance. You modify your physical appearance, choosing details such as height, build, facial traits, voice, hair, coloration, and other features. You may appear as a member of another species, but none of your statistics change. You cannot change your size category or basic body structure. You may use a Magic action to alter your appearance again during the spell.

Natural Weapons. You grow claws (Slashing), fangs (Piercing), horns (Piercing), or hooves (Bludgeoning). When you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike using this new feature, it deals 1d6 damage of the listed type, and you use your spellcasting ability for attack and damage rolls instead of Strength.

Name: Animal Friendship
Components: V, S, M (a morsel of food)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric
Text: Choose a Beast you can see within range. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed by you for the duration. If you or your allies harm the Beast, the spell ends immediately.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional Beast for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Animal Messenger
Components: V, S, M (a morsel of food)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
A Tiny Beast you can see within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be compelled to carry a message for you (Beasts with a Challenge Rating above 0 automatically succeed). You name a destination you have visited and describe the intended recipient in general terms, such as "a guard in uniform" or "a red-haired dwarf in a pointed hat." You also give the Beast a message of up to twenty-five words. It travels toward the location for the duration, covering about 25 miles per day, or 50 miles if it can fly.

When it arrives, the Beast delivers the message by mimicking your voice. If it fails to reach the destination before the spell ends, the message is lost and the Beast returns to where you cast the spell.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The spell's duration increases by 48 hours for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Animal Shapes
Text:
Choose any number of willing creatures you can see within range. Each one transforms into a Large or smaller Beast of your choice with a Challenge Rating of 4 or lower. You may select a different form for each creature. On later turns, you may use a Magic action to change their forms again.

A creature’s game statistics are replaced by those of the chosen Beast, but it keeps its creature type, Hit Points, Hit Dice, alignment, ability to communicate, and its Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. Its actions are limited by the Beast’s anatomy, and it cannot cast spells. Its equipment merges into the new form and cannot be used.

Each creature gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the first Beast form you assign to it. These Temporary Hit Points disappear when the spell ends. The transformation lasts for the duration or until the creature ends it with a Bonus Action.

Name: Animate Dead
Components: V, S, M (a drop of blood, a piece of flesh, and a pinch of bone dust)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin
Text:
Choose a pile of bones or the corpse of a Medium or Small Humanoid within range. The target becomes an Undead under your control: a Skeleton if you chose bones, or a Zombie if you chose a corpse.

On each of your turns, you may use a Bonus Action to mentally command any creature you animated with this spell, provided it is within 60 feet of you. If you control multiple Undead, you may issue the same command to all of them. You decide their actions and movement for their next turn. If you give no orders, they take the Dodge action and move only to avoid harm. Once commanded, they continue following the order until it is completed.

The Undead obey you for 24 hours. To maintain control for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell again on the creature before the current duration expires. Doing so reasserts control over up to four Undead you created with this spell.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You animate or reassert control over two additional Undead creatures for each spell slot level above 3. Each of the creatures must come from a different corpse or pile of bones.

Name: Animate Objects
Subclasses: Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric
Text:
You imbue nearby objects with motion. Choose a number of nonmagical objects within range that aren’t worn, carried, fixed in place, or Gargantuan. You may animate a number of objects equal to your spellcasting ability modifier; Medium or smaller objects count as one, Large objects count as two, and Huge objects count as three.

Each object sprouts limbs and becomes a Construct using the Animated Object stat block. The creatures remain under your control until the spell ends or until they drop to 0 Hit Points. They act as your allies, share your Initiative count, and take their turns immediately after yours.

While the spell lasts, you may use a Bonus Action to mentally command any animated creature within 500 feet of you. If you control multiple creatures, you may issue the same command to all of them. If you give no orders, they take the Dodge action and move only to avoid harm. When an animated creature reaches 0 Hit Points, it reverts to its original object form, and any excess damage carries over to that form.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The creature's Slam damage increases by 1d4 (Medium or smaller), 1d6 (Large), or 1d12 (Huge) for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Antilife Shell
Subclasses: Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock
Text:
A 10-foot aura radiates from you for the duration, forming a barrier that creatures other than Constructs and Undead cannot cross or reach through. Affected creatures can still cast spells or make attacks with Ranged or Reach weapons through the barrier.

If you move in a way that forces a creature to pass through the barrier, the spell immediately ends.

Name: Antimagic Field
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
A 10-foot aura of antimagic surrounds you. Within it, no one can cast spells, take Magic actions, or produce magical effects, and such effects cannot target or influence anything inside the aura. Magic items lose their magical properties while inside.

Areas of effect from spells or magical abilities cannot enter the aura, and teleportation or planar travel into or out of it is impossible. Portals close temporarily while inside the field.

Ongoing spells—except those created by an Artifact or a deity—are suppressed while in the aura. Suppressed effects do not function, but the suppressed time still counts against their duration.

Dispel Magic cannot affect the aura, and multiple Antimagic Fields do not cancel each other.

Name: Antipathy/Sympathy
Components: V, S, M (a mix of vinegar and honey)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
When casting the spell, choose whether it creates antipathy or sympathy, then target one Huge or smaller creature or object. You also specify a type of creature, such as red dragons, goblins, or vampires. A creature of that type must make a Wisdom saving throw when it comes within 120 feet of the target. The chosen effect determines the outcome on a failed save:

Antipathy. The creature becomes Frightened and must use its movement each turn to flee from the target by the safest route.

Sympathy. The creature becomes Charmed and must use its movement each turn to approach the target by the safest route. If it reaches within 5 feet of the target, it cannot willingly move away. If the target harms the Charmed creature, that creature may make a Wisdom saving throw to end the effect.

Ending the Effect. If the Frightened or Charmed creature ends its turn more than 120 feet from the target, it makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a success, the effect ends for that creature. A creature that succeeds is immune to the effect for 1 minute before it can be affected again.

Name: Arcane Eye
Components: V, S, M (a bit of bat fur)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger
Text:
You conjure an Invisible, indestructible eye at a point within range, where it hovers for the duration. You perceive through the eye, which sees in all directions and has 30 feet of Darkvision.

As a Bonus Action, you can move the eye up to 30 feet in any direction. Solid barriers block its movement, but it can slip through openings as small as 1 inch wide.

Name: Arcane Gate
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You create a pair of linked teleportation portals. Choose two Large, unoccupied ground spaces you can see: one within range and the other within 10 feet of you. A circular portal appears in each space and lasts for the duration.

Each portal is a flat, glowing ring filled with sight-blocking mist, hovering just above the ground and standing perpendicular to it.

Each portal is open on only one side (your choice). Anything entering the open side of one portal exits from the open side of the other as though the two spaces were adjacent. As a Bonus Action, you can change which side of each portal is open.

Name: Arcane Lock
Components: V, S, M (gold dust worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You touch a closed door, window, gate, container, or hatch and magically seal it for the duration. The lock cannot be opened by nonmagical means. You and any creatures you designate when casting the spell can open and close it normally. You may also set a password that, when spoken within 5 feet of the object, unlocks it for 1 minute.

Name: Arcane Vigor
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You draw upon your own vitality to heal yourself. Roll one or two of your unspent Hit Point Dice and regain Hit Points equal to the total rolled plus your spellcasting ability modifier. The dice you roll are then expended.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The number of unexpended Hit Dice you can roll increases by one for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Armor of Agatys
Components: V, S, M (a shard of blue glass)
Subclasses: Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin
Text: A layer of protective frost envelops you. You gain 5 Temporary Hit Points. If a creature hits you with a melee attack before the spell ends, it takes 5 Cold damage. The spell ends early if you lose all your Temporary Hit Points.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Temporary Hit Points and the Cold damage both increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Arms of Hadarc
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer
Text: Calling upon Hadarc, you unleash shadowy tendrils from your body. Each creature in a 10-foot Emanation centered on you must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d6 Necrotic damage and cannot take Reactions until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half damage and suffers no further effects.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Astral Projection
Components: V, S, M (for each of the spell's targets, one jacinth worth 1,000+ GP and one silver bar worth 100+ GP, all of which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to eight willing creatures within range project your astral forms into the Astral Plane (the spell ends immediately if you are already there). Each target’s physical body remains behind in suspended animation: it is Unconscious, does not need food or air, and does not age.

An astral form mirrors the target’s body and equipment, except for a silvery cord extending from between its shoulder blades. The cord becomes invisible after the first foot. If the cord is ever severed—only possible when an effect explicitly states so—the target’s body and astral form both die.

An astral form can travel freely through the Astral Plane. When it leaves that plane, the target’s body and possessions travel along the silver cord, causing the target to re-enter its body on the new plane.

Damage or effects that apply to an astral form do not affect the physical body, and vice versa. If either the body or astral form drops to 0 Hit Points, the spell ends for that target. The spell ends for all targets if you use a Magic action to dismiss it.

When the spell ends for a target that is still alive, it returns to its body and awakens from suspended animation.

Name: Augury
Components: V, S, M (specially marked sticks, bones, cards, or other divinatory tokens worth 25+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Ancestral Guardian (XGE) Barbarian, Ancestral Guardian (XGE) Barbarian
Text:
You receive an omen from an otherworldly force regarding the outcome of an action you plan to take within the next 30 minutes. The DM provides the omen using the Omens table.

Omens
Omen — For Results That Are…
Weal — Good
Woe — Bad
Weal and woe — Mixed
Indifference — Neither good nor bad

The omen does not account for changing circumstances, including other spells.

If you cast this spell more than once before completing a Long Rest, each casting after the first has a cumulative 25 percent chance of producing no answer.

Name: Aura of Life
Subclasses: Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: An aura extends from you in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. While inside the aura, you and your allies have Resistance to Necrotic damage, and your Hit Point maximum cannot be reduced. If an ally at 0 Hit Points starts its turn in the aura, it regains 1 Hit Point.

Name: Aura of Purity
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric
Text: An aura extends from you in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. While inside the aura, you and your allies have Resistance to Poison damage and Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end effects that impose the Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned condition.

Name: Aura of Vitality
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text: An aura extends from you in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. When the aura appears and at the start of each of your turns, you can restore 2d6 Hit Points to one creature within it.

Name: Awaken
Components: V, S, M (an agate worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Text:
You spend the casting time tracing magical patterns into a precious gemstone, then touch the target. The target must be a Beast or Plant creature with Intelligence 3 or lower, or a noncreature plant. The target’s Intelligence becomes 10, and it gains the ability to speak one language you know. If the target is a natural plant, it becomes a Plant creature, gains mobility through its limbs, roots, or vines, and gains senses similar to a humanoid’s. The DM chooses appropriate statistics, such as those of an Awakened Shrub or Awakened Tree.

The awakened creature is Charmed by you for 30 days or until you or your allies harm it. When the Charmed condition ends, the creature decides its attitude toward you.

Name: Bane
Components: V, S, M (a drop of blood)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Up to three creatures you can see within range must each make a Charisma saving throw. A creature that fails subtracts 1d4 from every attack roll and saving throw it makes until the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Banishing Smite
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: The target hit by the attack takes an additional 5d10 Force damage. If this reduces the target to 50 Hit Points or fewer, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration. While there, it is Incapacitated. When the spell ends, it reappears in the space it left or the nearest unoccupied space.

Name: Banishment
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin
Text:
One creature you can see within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be transported to a harmless demiplane for the duration. While there, it is Incapacitated. When the spell ends, it reappears in the space it left or the nearest unoccupied space.

If the target is an Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, or Fiend, and the spell lasts the full minute, it does not return. Instead, it is transported to a random location on a plane associated with its creature type (DM’s choice).
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Barkskin
Components: V, S, M (a handful of oak bark)
Subclasses: Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric
Text: You touch a willing creature. For the duration, the target’s skin takes on a bark-like texture, and its Armor Class becomes 17 if it was lower.

Name: Beacon of Hope
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Choose any number of creatures within range. For the duration, each target has Advantage on Wisdom saving throws and Death Saving Throws and regains the maximum possible number of Hit Points from any healing.

Name: Beast Sense
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Totem Warrior (PHB'14) Barbarian, Wild Heart (PHB'24) Barbarian
Text: You touch a willing Beast, linking your senses to it for the duration. While doing so, you can perceive everything the Beast perceives, benefiting from any special senses it possesses.

Name: Befuddlement
Components: V, S, M (a key ring with no keys)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You assault the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target makes an Intelligence saving throw.

On a failed save, it takes 10d12 Psychic damage and becomes unable to cast spells or take the Magic action. At the end of every 30 days, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success. Greater Restoration, Heal, or Wish can also end the effect.

On a successful save, the target takes only half the damage.

Name: Bestow Curse
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You touch a creature, which must make a Wisdom saving throw or become cursed for the duration. While cursed, the target suffers one of the following effects of your choice:

Choose one ability; the target has Disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws using that ability.
The target has Disadvantage on attack rolls made against you.
At the start of each of its turns in combat, the target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be forced to take the Dodge action that turn.
If you deal damage to the target with an attack or spell, it takes an additional 1d8 Necrotic damage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you cast this spell using a level 4 spell slot, you can maintain Concentration on it for up to 10 minutes. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, the spell doesn't require Concentration, and the duration becomes 8 hours (level 5-6 slot) or 24 hours (level 7-8 slot). If you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until dispelled.

Name: Giant Spectral Hand
Components: V, S, M (an eggshell and a glove)
Subclasses: Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You conjure a Large, shimmering hand of magical force in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The hand lasts for the duration and moves at your command, mirroring your own hand’s motions.

The hand is an object with AC 20 and Hit Points equal to your Hit Point maximum. If reduced to 0 Hit Points, the spell ends. The hand does not occupy its space.

When you cast the spell and as a Bonus Action on later turns, you can move the hand up to 60 feet and cause one of the following effects:

Clenched Fist. The hand punches a creature within 5 feet. Make a melee spell attack; on a hit, the target takes 5d8 Force damage.

Forceful Hand. The hand attempts to shove a Huge or smaller creature within 5 feet. The target must make a Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet plus an additional distance equal to five times your spellcasting ability modifier. The hand moves with the target, staying within 5 feet.

Grasping Hand. The hand tries to grapple a Huge or smaller creature within 5 feet. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw or become Grappled (escape DC equals your spell save DC). While grappling, you can use a Bonus Action to crush the target, dealing 4d6 + your spellcasting ability modifier Bludgeoning damage.

Interposing Hand. The hand provides you with Half Cover against attacks or effects originating from its space or passing through it. Its space is also Difficult Terrain for your enemies.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage of the Clenched Fist increases by 2d8 and the damage of the Grasping Hand increases by 2d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Blade Barrier
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You create a wall of spinning, magical blades. The wall appears within range and lasts for the duration. You can form a straight wall up to 100 feet long, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick, or a circular wall up to 60 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick. The wall grants Three-Quarters Cover, and its space is Difficult Terrain.

Any creature in the wall’s space makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d10 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature also makes this save if it enters the wall’s space or ends its turn there, but no more than once per turn.

Name: Blade Ward
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Scion of the Three (FRHoF) Rogue
Text: Whenever a creature makes an attack roll against you before the spell ends, it subtracts 1d4 from the attack roll.

Name: Bless
Components: V, S, M (a Holy Symbol worth 5+ GP)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You bless up to three creatures within range. Until the spell ends, whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw, it adds 1d4 to the result.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Blight

Alternatively, you may target a nonmagical plant that is not a creature, such as a tree or shrub. It does not make a save; it simply withers and dies.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The extra damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

Text:
At the end of each of your turns for the duration, roll 1d6. On a 4–6, you slip out of your current plane and appear on the Ethereal Plane (the spell ends immediately if you are already there). While on the Ethereal Plane, you can see the plane you left in muted gray tones, but nothing beyond 60 feet. You can interact only with creatures on the Ethereal Plane, and creatures on the other plane cannot perceive you unless they have a special ability that allows it.

You return to the other plane at the start of your next turn and when the spell ends if you are still on the Ethereal Plane. You reappear in an unoccupied space you can see within 10 feet of where you vanished. If no such space exists, you appear in the nearest available space.

Flammable objects in the area that aren’t worn or carried ignite.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Text:
A storm cloud forms above you at a point you can see within range, creating a 10-foot-tall Cylinder with a 60-foot radius.

When you cast the spell, choose a point beneath the cloud. A lightning bolt strikes that point, and each creature within 5 feet of it makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Until the spell ends, you can use a Magic action to call down another bolt, targeting the same point or a different one.

If you cast this spell outdoors during a storm, you seize control of the existing storm instead of creating a new one, and the spell’s damage increases by 1d10.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 3.

Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
Each Humanoid in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, choose one of the following effects for that creature:

The creature becomes Immune to the Charmed and Frightened conditions for the duration. If it was already Charmed or Frightened, those conditions are suppressed.
The creature becomes Indifferent toward creatures you choose that it was previously Hostile toward. This indifference ends if the creature takes damage or sees its allies harmed. When the spell ends, its attitude returns to normal.

Each target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. One additional bolt leaps from the first target to another target for each spell slot level above 6.

When the spell ends, it realizes it was Charmed.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

Text: One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw, with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. When the spell ends, it knows it was Charmed.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin
Text:
You throw an orb of elemental energy at a creature within range. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder for the orb’s type, then make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 3d8 damage of the chosen type.

If two or more of the d8s show the same number, the orb jumps to a different target of your choice within 30 feet of the original target. Make a new attack roll and damage roll for the new target. The orb cannot jump again unless you cast this spell using a level 2 or higher spell slot.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1. The orb can leap a maximum number of times equal to the level of the slot expended, and a creature can be targeted only once by each casting of this spell.

Name: Circle of Death
Components: V, S, M (the powder of a crushed black pearl worth 500+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric

Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric

Name: Clairvoyance

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Ancestral Guardian (XGE) Barbarian, Ancestral Guardian (XGE) Barbarian, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You create an Invisible sensor at a point within range that is either familiar to you (a place you’ve visited or seen) or an obvious but unfamiliar location (like behind a door or around a corner). The intangible, invulnerable sensor remains for the duration.

When you cast the spell, choose seeing or hearing. You can perceive through the sensor using the chosen sense as though you were in its space. As a Bonus Action, you can switch between the two senses.

A creature capable of seeing Invisible objects (such as with See Invisibility or Truesight) perceives the sensor as a faint, fist-sized orb of light.

Name: Clone
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes, and a sealable vessel worth 2,000+ GP that is large enough to hold the creature being cloned)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You touch a creature or at least 1 cubic inch of its flesh. A dormant duplicate of that creature forms inside the vessel used for the spell and finishes growing after 120 days; you choose whether the clone matches the creature’s age or is younger. The clone remains inert indefinitely as long as the vessel is undisturbed.

If the original creature dies after the clone is complete, its soul transfers to the clone if the soul is free and willing. The clone is physically identical to the original and retains its personality, memories, and abilities, but none of its equipment. The original remains, if any, become inert and cannot be revived, as the soul now resides in the clone.

Name: Cloud of Daggers
Components: V, S, M (a sliver of glass)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter

You summon spinning daggers in a 5-foot Cube centered on a point within range. Each creature in the Cube takes 4d4 Slashing damage. A creature also takes this damage if it enters the Cube, ends its turn there, or if the Cube moves into its space. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

On later turns, you can use a Magic action to teleport the Cube up to 30 feet.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 2.

Text:
You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of yellow-green fog centered on a point within range. The fog lasts for the duration or until dispersed by strong wind (such as Gust of Wind). The area is Heavily Obscured.

Each creature in the Sphere must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 5d8 Poison damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature must also make this save when the Sphere moves into its space, or when it enters the Sphere or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

At the start of each of your turns, the Sphere drifts 10 feet away from you.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer

Text:
You speak a single-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or obey the command on its next turn. Choose one of the following commands:

Approach. The target moves toward you by the most direct route, ending its turn if it comes within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest route.

Grovel. The target falls Prone and ends its turn.

Halt. The target does not move and takes no action or Bonus Action on its turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Commune
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You reach out to a deity or divine intermediary and ask up to three yes-or-no questions before the spell ends. You receive a truthful answer to each.

Divine beings are not all-knowing, so you may receive “unclear” if the question concerns something beyond the deity’s knowledge. If a one-word answer would be misleading or contrary to the deity’s interests, the DM may provide a brief phrase instead.

If you cast this spell more than once before finishing a Long Rest, each casting after the first has a cumulative 25 percent chance of yielding no answer.

Name: Commune with Nature
Subclasses: Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Totem Warrior (PHB'14) Barbarian, Wild Heart (PHB'24) Barbarian
Text:
You commune with nature spirits to learn about the surrounding area. Outdoors, the spell reveals information about the region within 3 miles of you; in caves or natural underground areas, the radius is 300 feet. The spell does not function in areas dominated by construction, such as towns or fortresses.

Choose three of the following facts; you learn them as they apply to the area:

• Locations of settlements
• Locations of portals to other planes
• Location of one Challenge Rating 10+ creature (DM’s choice) that is a Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead
• The most common type of plant, mineral, or Beast (your choice)
• Locations of bodies of water

For example, you might learn the location of a powerful monster, the nearest water sources, and the positions of nearby towns.

Name: Compelled Duel
Subclasses: Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin
Text:
You attempt to draw a creature into a duel. One creature you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and it cannot willingly move to a space more than 30 feet away from you.

The spell ends if you attack a creature other than the target, cast a spell on an enemy other than the target, if an ally damages the target, or if you end your turn more than 30 feet from it.

Name: Comprehend Languages
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of soot and salt)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Banneret (FRHoF) Fighter, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric
Text: For the duration, you comprehend the literal meaning of any spoken or signed language you perceive. You can also understand any written language you see, provided you touch the surface on which it is written. Reading a page takes about 1 minute. This spell does not decipher codes or hidden messages.

Name: Compulsion
Subclasses: Glory (TCE) Paladin, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed until the spell ends.

While the spell lasts, you can use a Bonus Action to choose a horizontal direction. Each Charmed creature must use as much movement as possible on its next turn to move in that direction by the safest route. After moving, the creature repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

Name: Cone of Cold
Components: V, S, M (a small crystal or glass cone)
Subclasses: Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Winter Walker (FRHoF) Ranger
Text: You release a surge of freezing wind. Each creature in a 60-foot Cone originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 Cold damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature slain by this spell becomes a frozen statue until it thaws.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Confusion
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric
Text:
Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature cannot take Bonus Actions or Reactions and must roll 1d10 at the start of each of its turns to determine its behavior:

1 — The creature takes no action and uses all its movement in a random cardinal direction (roll 1d4: 1 north, 2 east, 3 south, 4 west).
2–6 — The creature takes no actions and does not move.
7–8 — The creature does not move and makes one melee attack against a random creature within reach; if none are in reach, it takes no action.
9–10 — The creature chooses its own behavior.

At the end of each of its turns, the creature repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Sphere's radius increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Conjure Animals
Subclasses: Moon (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
You summon nature spirits that take the form of a Large pack of spectral, intangible animals in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The pack lasts for the duration, and you choose its appearance—wolves, serpents, birds, and so on.

While within 5 feet of the pack, you have Advantage on Strength saving throws. When you move on your turn, you can also move the pack up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

Whenever the pack moves within 10 feet of a creature you can see, or whenever a creature enters a space within 10 feet of the pack or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d10 Slashing damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Conjure Barrage
Components: V, S, M (a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP)
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer
Text: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and conjure spectral copies of it—or matching ammunition—that fly forward before vanishing. Each creature of your choice in a 60-foot Cone must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Conjure Celestial
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You summon a spirit from the Upper Planes that appears as a pillar of radiant light in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. For each creature you can see in the Cylinder, choose one of the following lights:

Healing Light. The creature regains Hit Points equal to 4d12 + your spellcasting ability modifier.

Searing Light. The creature makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d12 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

The Cylinder sheds Bright Light, and when you move on your turn, you can also move the Cylinder up to 30 feet.

Whenever the Cylinder moves into a creature’s space, or whenever a creature enters or ends its turn in the Cylinder, you may apply one of the lights. A creature can be affected only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing and damage increase by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 7.

Name: Conjure Elemental
Subclasses: Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid
Text:
You summon a Large, intangible elemental spirit in an unoccupied space within range. Choose air (Lightning), earth (Thunder), fire (Fire), or water (Cold) to determine its damage type. The spirit lasts for the duration.

Whenever a creature you can see enters the spirit’s space or starts its turn within 5 feet of it, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw if the spirit has no creature Restrained. On a failed save, the creature takes 8d8 damage of the spirit’s type and becomes Restrained until the spell ends.

At the start of each of its turns, a Restrained creature repeats the saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 4d8 damage of the spirit’s type; on a success, it is no longer Restrained.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Conjure Fey
Text:
You summon a Medium Fey spirit in an unoccupied space you can see within range. It resembles a Fey creature of your choice and lasts for the duration. When it appears, you may make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 3d12 + your spellcasting ability modifier Psychic damage and becomes Frightened of both you and the spirit until the start of your next turn.

As a Bonus Action on later turns, you can teleport the spirit to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of where it was, then make the attack against a creature within 5 feet of its new position.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Conjure Minor Elementals
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin
Text:
You summon elemental spirits that swirl around you in a 15-foot Emanation for the duration. Whenever you hit a creature inside the Emanation with an attack, the attack deals an extra 2d8 damage. The damage type is Acid, Cold, Fire, or Lightning, chosen each time you hit.

Additionally, the ground within the Emanation is Difficult Terrain for your enemies.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Conjure Volley
Components: V, S, M (a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP)
Text: You raise the weapon used to cast the spell and choose a point within range. Hundreds of spectral copies of that weapon—or matching ammunition—rain down in a massive volley before vanishing. Each creature of your choice in a 40-foot-radius, 20-foot-high Cylinder centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d8 Force damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Name: Conjure Woodland Beings
Text:
You call forth nature spirits that drift around you in a 10-foot Emanation for the duration. Whenever the Emanation enters a creature’s space, or whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 5d8 Force damage, or half as much on a success. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

For the duration, you can also take the Disengage action as a Bonus Action.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Contact Other Plane
Subclasses: Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin
Text:
You reach out to a demigod, ancient sage, or otherworldly intellect. Such contact risks overwhelming your mind. When you cast the spell, make a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw. On a success, you may ask the entity up to five questions before the spell ends. The DM answers each with a single word—such as “yes,” “no,” “maybe,” “never,” “irrelevant,” or “unclear.” If a one-word answer would mislead, the DM may instead give a short phrase.

On a failed save, you take 6d6 Psychic damage and become Incapacitated until you finish a Long Rest. A Greater Restoration spell ends this effect.

Name: Contagion
Subclasses: Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text:
Your touch delivers a magical disease. The target must make a Constitution saving throw or take 11d8 Necrotic damage and become Poisoned. Choose one ability when you cast the spell; while Poisoned, the target has Disadvantage on saving throws using that ability.

At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the saving throw. If it succeeds three times, the spell ends. If it fails three times, the spell lasts for 7 days.

If an effect would end the Poisoned condition on the target, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or the condition persists.

Name: Contingency
Components: V, S, M (a gem-encrusted statuette of yourself worth 1,500+ GP)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
Choose a spell of 5th level or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of one action, and that can target only you. You cast that spell—called the contingent spell—at the same time as Contingency, expending spell slots for both. The contingent spell does not take effect immediately; instead, it activates when a trigger you describe occurs. For example, a Contingency paired with Water Breathing might trigger when you are submerged in water.

The contingent spell activates instantly the first time the trigger occurs, whether or not you want it to, and Contingency then ends.

The contingent spell affects only you, even if it normally targets others. You can have only one Contingency active at a time; casting this spell again ends the previous one. Contingency also ends if its material component is ever not on your person.

Name: Continual Flame
Components: V, S, M (ruby dust worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: A flame appears on an object you touch. It sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The flame looks real but produces no heat and consumes no fuel. It can be covered or hidden but cannot be smothered or extinguished.

Name: Control Water
Components: V, S, M (a mixture of water and dust)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Until the spell ends, you control any water within a Cube up to 100 feet on a side, choosing one of the following effects. As a Magic action on later turns, you may repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

Flood. You raise the level of all standing water in the area by up to 20 feet. If cast on a large body of water, you instead create a 20-foot-tall wave that travels across the area and crashes. Huge or smaller vehicles caught in the wave are carried with it, and any such vehicle struck has a 25 percent chance of capsizing.

The elevated water level remains until the spell ends or you choose another effect. If a wave was created, it repeats at the start of your next turn while this effect lasts.

Part Water. You split the water in the area, forming a trench with walls of water on either side. The trench remains until the spell ends or you choose another effect, after which the water refills over the next round.

Redirect Flow. You change the direction of flowing water in the area, even forcing it over obstacles or up walls. Once the water leaves the spell’s area, it resumes its natural course. The redirected flow persists until the spell ends or you choose another effect.

Whirlpool. You create a whirlpool in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 feet square and 25 feet deep. The whirlpool is 5 feet wide at the base, up to 50 feet wide at the top, and 25 feet tall. It lasts until you choose another effect or the spell ends. Any creature in the water within 25 feet of the whirlpool is pulled 10 feet toward it. A creature entering or ending its turn in the whirlpool must make a Strength saving throw, taking 2d8 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature can escape only by taking an action to swim away and succeeding on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC.

Name: Control Weather
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You seize control of the weather within 5 miles for the duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell, and it ends early if you go indoors.

When you cast the spell, you alter the current weather—precipitation, temperature, and wind—based on the DM’s starting conditions. It takes 1d4 × 10 minutes for the new conditions to take effect. Once they do, you may change them again. When the spell ends, the weather gradually returns to normal.

To change the weather, find the current stage on the tables and shift it up or down by one step. When altering wind, you may also change its direction.

Precipitation
1 Clear
2 Light clouds
3 Overcast or ground fog
4 Rain, hail, or snow
5 Torrential rain, driving hail, or blizzard

Temperature
1 Heat wave
2 Hot
3 Warm
4 Cool
5 Cold
6 Freezing

Wind
1 Calm
2 Moderate wind
3 Strong wind
4 Gale
5 Storm

Name: Cordon of Arrows
Components: V, S, M (four or more arrows or bolts)
Text:
You touch up to four nonmagical Arrows or Bolts and plant them in the ground in your space. Until the spell ends, the ammunition cannot be physically removed. Whenever a creature other than you enters a space within 30 feet of the ammunition for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, one piece of ammunition launches at it. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d4 Piercing damage, after which that piece of ammunition is destroyed. The spell ends when no ammunition remains.

When you cast the spell, you may designate creatures that the spell ignores.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The amount of ammunition that can be affected increases by two for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Counterspell
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer
Text: You attempt to disrupt a creature as it casts a spell. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell fizzles with no effect, and the action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted. If the spell was cast using a spell slot, that slot is not expended.

Name: Create Food and Water
Subclasses: Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text: You create 45 pounds of food and 30 gallons of fresh water on the ground or in containers within range—enough to stave off hunger and dehydration. The food is plain but nourishing and resembles any type of food you choose, and the water is clean. Any uneaten food spoils after 24 hours.

Name: Create Undead
Components: V, S, M (one 150+ GP black onyx stone for each corpse)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small Humanoids within range. Each corpse becomes a Ghoul under your control (see the Monster Manual for its stat block).

As a Bonus Action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell, provided it is within 120 feet of you. If you control multiple creatures, you may issue the same command to all of them. You decide what actions they take and where they move on their next turn, or you may give a general order such as guarding an area. If you give no commands, they take the Dodge action and move only to avoid harm. Once commanded, they continue following the order until it is completed.

The Ghouls obey you for 24 hours. To maintain control for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on them again before the current duration ends. Doing so reasserts control over up to three Ghouls you previously animated instead of creating new ones.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you use a level 7 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over four Ghouls. If you use a level 8 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over five Ghouls or two Ghasts or Wights. If you use a level 9 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over six Ghouls, three Ghasts or Wights, or two Mummies. See the Monster Manual for these stat blocks.

Name: Create or Destroy Water
Components: V, S, M (a mix of water and sand)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You choose one of the following effects:

Create Water. You create up to 10 gallons of clean water within range in an open container. Alternatively, the water falls as rain in a 30-foot Cube within range, extinguishing exposed flames.

Destroy Water. You destroy up to 10 gallons of water in an open container within range. Alternatively, you disperse fog in a 30-foot Cube within range.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You create or destroy 10 additional gallons of water, or the size of the Cube increases by 5 feet, for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Creation
Subclasses: Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You draw shadowy essence from the Shadowfell to form an object within range. The object may be made of vegetable matter (cloth, rope, wood, etc.) or mineral matter (stone, crystal, metal, etc.). It must fit within a 5-foot Cube and must be a form and material you have seen before.

The spell’s duration depends on the material, as shown in the Materials table. If the object contains multiple materials, use the shortest duration. If the object is used as a Material component for another spell, that spell automatically fails.

Materials
Vegetable matter — 24 hours
Stone or crystal — 12 hours
Precious metals — 1 hour
Gems — 10 minutes
Adamantine or mithral — 1 minute
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Cube increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Crown of Madness
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin
Text:
One creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. The creature automatically succeeds if it is not Humanoid.

A spectral crown appears above the Charmed creature’s head. Before moving on each of its turns, it must use its action to make a melee attack against a creature you mentally choose. If you choose no creature or none are within reach, it acts normally. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

On later turns, you must use the Magic action to maintain control of the target, or the spell ends.

Name: Crusader's Mantle
Subclasses: War (PHB'14) Cleric, War (PHB'24) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You emit a magical aura in a 30-foot Emanation. While inside the aura, you and your allies deal an extra 1d4 Radiant damage when hitting with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

Name: Cure Wounds
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Moon (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text: You touch a creature, restoring Hit Points equal to 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Dancing Lights
Components: V, S, M (a bit of phosphorus)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create up to four torch-sized lights within range. They may appear as torches, lanterns, or floating orbs. Alternatively, you may merge them into a single Medium-sized, vaguely humanoid glowing form. Each light sheds Dim Light in a 10-foot radius.

As a Bonus Action, you can move the lights up to 60 feet to a space within range. Each light must remain within 20 feet of another light created by this spell, and any light that moves beyond the spell’s range disappears.

Name: Darkness
Components: V, M (bat fur and a piece of coal)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Shadow (XGE) Sorcerer, Shadow (XGE) Sorcerer, Shadow (PHB'24) Monk, Shadow (PHB'14) Monk
Text:
For the duration, magical Darkness spreads from a point within range, filling a 15-foot-radius Sphere. Darkvision cannot see through it, and nonmagical light cannot illuminate it.

Alternatively, you may cast the spell on an object not being worn or carried, causing the Darkness to fill a 15-foot Emanation from that object. Covering the object with something opaque blocks the Darkness.

If any part of this spell’s area overlaps with an area of Bright or Dim Light created by a spell of level 2 or lower, that spell is dispelled.

Name: Darkvision
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Shadow (PHB'14) Monk
Text: For the duration, a willing creature you touch gains Darkvision out to 150 feet.

Name: Daylight
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text:
Sunlight radiates from a point you choose within range, filling a 60-foot-radius Sphere with Bright Light and shedding Dim Light for an additional 60 feet.

Alternatively, you can cast the spell on an object not being worn or carried, causing the sunlight to emanate in a 60-foot radius from that object. Covering the object with something opaque blocks the light.

If any part of this spell’s area overlaps with magical Darkness created by a spell of level 3 or lower, that spell is dispelled.

Name: Death Ward
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You touch a creature and grant it protection against death. The first time the target would drop to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, it instead drops to 1 Hit Point, and the spell ends.

If the spell is still active when an effect would instantly kill the target without dealing damage, that effect is negated for the target, and the spell ends.

Name: Delayed Blast Fireball
Components: V, S, M (a ball of bat guano and sulfur)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A beam of yellow light shoots from you and condenses into a glowing bead at a point you choose within range. The bead remains for the duration. When the spell ends, the bead explodes, and each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes Fire damage equal to the total accumulated damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

The spell begins with 12d6 Fire damage, and the damage increases by 1d6 at the end of each of your turns while the spell persists.

If a creature touches the bead before the spell ends, it must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the bead explodes. On a success, the creature may throw the bead up to 40 feet. If the bead enters a creature’s space or strikes a solid surface, it explodes.

When the bead detonates, unattended flammable objects in the area ignite.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The base damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 7.

Name: Demiplane
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You create a shadowy Medium-sized door on a flat solid surface you can see within range. The door opens into a demiplane: an empty 30-foot-square room made of wood or stone (your choice).

When the spell ends, the door disappears. Objects inside the demiplane remain there. Creatures inside remain unless they choose to exit as the door vanishes, appearing Prone in the nearest unoccupied spaces to where the door was.

Each time you cast this spell, you may create a new demiplane or connect the door to one you previously created. If you know the nature and contents of a demiplane created by another creature’s casting of this spell, you may connect to that demiplane instead.

Name: Destructive Wave
Subclasses: Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Destructive energy bursts outward from you in a 30-foot Emanation. Each creature you choose within the area must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 5d6 Thunder damage and 5d6 Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice) and falls Prone. On a successful save, it takes half damage and does not fall Prone.

Name: Detect Evil and Good
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text:
For the duration, you sense the location of any Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead within 30 feet of you. You also detect whether a Hallow spell is active in that area and where it is located.

This spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

Name: Detect Magic
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin
Text:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of you. If you detect such effects, you may use the Magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object bearing magic. If the effect was created by a spell, you learn its school of magic.

This spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

Name: Detect Poison and Disease
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
For the duration, you sense the location of poisons, poisonous or venomous creatures, and magical contagions within 30 feet of you. You also learn the type of poison, creature, or contagion in each case.

This spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

Name: Detect Thoughts
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You activate one of the following effects. Until the spell ends, you may activate either effect again as a Magic action on later turns.

Sense Thoughts. You detect the presence of thoughts within 30 feet of you from creatures that know languages or are telepathic. You do not read the thoughts, but you know a thinking creature is present.

This effect is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

Read Thoughts. Choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, or a creature within 30 feet that you detected with Sense Thoughts. You learn what is foremost in the creature’s mind. If the creature knows no languages and is not telepathic, you learn nothing.

As a Magic action on your next turn, you may probe deeper. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you learn its reasoning, emotions, and something significant occupying its thoughts. On a success, the spell ends. In either case, the target becomes aware of your intrusion. Until you shift your focus away, the target may use its action to make an Intelligence (Arcana) check against your spell save DC, ending the spell on a success.

Name: Dimension Door
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Fey Wanderer (TCE) Ranger, Fey Wanderer (PHB'24) Ranger, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You teleport to a destination within range, arriving precisely where you intend. The location may be one you can see, visualize, or describe by distance and direction, such as “200 feet straight down” or “300 feet northwest at a 45-degree angle.”

You may also teleport one willing creature within 5 feet of you; it appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of your destination.

If you or the other creature would arrive in a space occupied by a creature or completely filled by objects, each of you takes 4d6 Force damage, and the teleportation fails.

Name: Disguise Self
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Gloom Stalker (PHB'24) Ranger, Gloom Stalker (XGE) Ranger, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You alter your appearance—including clothing, armor, weapons, and carried items—for the duration. You may appear up to 1 foot shorter or taller and seem lighter or heavier, but you must retain the same basic limb arrangement. Beyond that, the illusion’s details are up to you.

The illusion does not withstand physical inspection. For example, if you appear to wear a hat, objects pass through it, and anyone touching it feels nothing.

A creature can discern the disguise only by taking the Study action and succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC.

Name: Disintegrate
Components: V, S, M (a lodestone and dust)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You fire a green ray at a target you can see within range. The target may be a creature, a nonmagical object, or a magical force construct such as a Wall of Force.

A creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10d6 + 40 Force damage. If this reduces it to 0 Hit Points, the creature and all nonmagical items it is wearing or carrying disintegrate into gray dust. Only True Resurrection or Wish can restore it.

A Large or smaller nonmagical object or magical force creation is automatically disintegrated. If the target is Huge or larger, a 10-foot-Cube portion of it is destroyed.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 3d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Dispel Evil and Good
Components: V, S, M (powdered silver and iron)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
For the duration, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead have Disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You may end the spell early by using one of the following options:

Break Enchantment. As a Magic action, you touch a creature possessed by or affected with the Charmed or Frightened condition caused by one of the listed creature types. The target is freed from those effects.

Dismissal. As a Magic action, you target a creature of one of the listed types within 5 feet. It must make a Charisma saving throw or be sent back to its home plane. If it is not on its home plane, Undead are sent to the Shadowfell and Fey to the Feywild.

Name: Dispel Magic
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Fey Wanderer (TCE) Ranger, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric
Text: Choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. Any ongoing spell of level 3 or lower on the target ends. For each spell of level 4 or higher affecting the target, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC 10 + the spell’s level). On a success, that spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You automatically end a spell on the target if the spell's level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you use.

Name: Dissonant Whispers
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text: You fill a creature’s mind with a jarring melody. One creature you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Psychic damage and must immediately use its Reaction, if available, to move as far from you as possible by the safest route. On a successful save, it takes half damage only.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Divination
Components: V, S, M (incense worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter
Text:
You contact a deity or divine servant and ask one question concerning a specific goal, event, or activity occurring within the next 7 days. The DM provides a truthful answer, which may be a short phrase or cryptic rhyme. The spell does not account for changing circumstances.

If you cast this spell more than once before finishing a Long Rest, each casting after the first has a cumulative 25 percent chance of producing no answer.

Name: Divine Favor
Subclasses: War (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Until the spell ends, your weapon attacks deal an additional 1d4 Radiant damage on a hit.

Name: Divine Smite
Text: The target takes an additional 2d8 Radiant damage from the attack. If the target is a Fiend or an Undead, the extra damage increases to 3d8.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Divine Word
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You speak a word infused with celestial power. Each creature of your choice within range makes a Charisma saving throw.
