On a failed save, a creature with 50 Hit Points or fewer suffers an effect based on its current Hit Points, as shown in the Divine Word table. Regardless of Hit Points, any Celestial, Elemental, Fey, or Fiend that fails its save is banished to its home plane (if not already there) and cannot return for 24 hours except via a Wish spell.

Divine Word Effects
Hit Points — Effect
0–20 — The creature dies.
21–30 — The creature is Blinded, Deafened, and Stunned for 1 hour.
31–40 — The creature is Blinded and Deafened for 10 minutes.
41–50 — The creature is Deafened for 1 minute.

Name: Dominate Beast
Subclasses: Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
One Beast you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. The target has Advantage on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

While you and the Charmed Beast are on the same plane, you share a telepathic link. On your turn, you may issue commands through this link (no action required), such as “Attack that creature,” “Move there,” or “Retrieve that object.” The Beast follows your instructions as best it can. If it completes an order and receives no new one, it acts as it wishes, prioritizing its own safety.

You may command the Beast to take a Reaction, but doing so requires your own Reaction.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 5 (up to 10 minutes), 6 (up to 1 hour), or 7+ (up to 8 hours).

Name: Dominate Monster
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
One creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. The target has Advantage on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

While you and the Charmed creature are on the same plane, you share a telepathic link. On your turn, you may issue commands through this link (no action required), such as “Attack that creature,” “Move there,” or “Retrieve that object.” The creature follows your instructions as best it can. If it completes an order and receives no new one, it acts as it wishes, focusing on self-preservation.

You may command the creature to take a Reaction, but doing so requires your own Reaction.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a level 9 spell slot (up to 8 hours).

Name: Dominate Person
Subclasses: Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
One Humanoid you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. The target has Advantage on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

While you and the Charmed Humanoid are on the same plane, you share a telepathic link. On your turn, you may issue commands through this link (no action required), such as “Attack that creature,” “Move there,” or “Retrieve that object.” The Humanoid follows your instructions as best it can. If it completes an order and receives no new one, it acts as it chooses, prioritizing its own safety.

You may command the Humanoid to take a Reaction, but doing so requires your own Reaction.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 6 (up to 10 minutes), 7 (up to 1 hour), or 8+ (up to 8 hours).

Name: Dragon's Breath
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You touch a willing creature and choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Until the spell ends, the target may use a Magic action to exhale a 15-foot Cone of that damage type. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 damage of the chosen type on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Instant Summons
Components: V, S, M (a sapphire worth 1,000+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You touch the sapphire used in the spell and an object weighing 10 pounds or less whose longest side is 6 feet or shorter. The spell places an Invisible mark on the object and inscribes its name onto the sapphire. Each casting requires a different sapphire.

Later, you may take a Magic action to speak the object’s name and crush the sapphire. The object instantly appears in your hand, regardless of distance or planar separation, and the spell ends.

If another creature is holding or carrying the object, crushing the sapphire does not teleport it. Instead, you learn the creature’s identity and current location.

Name: Dream
Components: V, S, M (a handful of sand)
Subclasses: Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You target a creature you know on the same plane. You or a willing creature you touch enters a trance to act as a dream messenger. While in the trance, the messenger is Incapacitated and has a Speed of 0.

If the target is asleep, the messenger appears in its dreams and may converse with it for the duration. The messenger may shape the dream’s environment, creating scenery, objects, and imagery. The messenger may end the trance at any time, ending the spell. The target remembers the dream clearly upon waking.

If the target is awake when the spell is cast, the messenger senses this and may either end the trance or wait until the target sleeps, at which point the messenger enters the dream.

You may choose to make the messenger terrifying. If you do, the messenger may deliver a message of up to ten words, after which the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target gains no benefit from its rest and takes 3d6 Psychic damage upon waking.

Name: Druidcraft
Subclasses: Arcane Archer (XGE) Fighter, Arcane Archer (XGE) Fighter, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Giant (BGG) Barbarian, Giant (BGG) Barbarian, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Calling upon nature spirits, you create one of the following effects within range:

Weather Sensor. You create a Tiny, harmless sign predicting the weather at your location for the next 24 hours—such as a glowing sun, a drifting cloud, or falling snowflakes. The effect lasts 1 round.

Bloom. You cause a flower to blossom, a seed pod to open, or a leaf bud to unfurl.

Sensory Effect. You create a harmless sensory display, such as swirling leaves, tiny spectral fey, a soft breeze, an animal sound, or a faint skunk odor. The effect must fit within a 5-foot Cube.

Fire Play. You ignite or extinguish a candle, torch, or campfire.

Name: Earthquake
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
Choose a point on the ground you can see within range. A violent tremor shakes the earth in a 100-foot-radius circle centered on that point for the duration. The ground becomes Difficult Terrain.

When you cast the spell and at the end of each of your turns, each creature on the ground in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature falls Prone and loses Concentration.

You may also create the following effects:

Fissures. At the end of the turn you cast the spell, 1d6 fissures open in the area at locations you choose (not beneath structures). Each fissure is 10 feet wide and 1d10 × 10 feet deep, stretching from one edge of the area to the opposite edge. A creature in a fissure’s space must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall in; on a success, it moves with the fissure’s edge.

Structures. Any structure touching the ground in the area takes 50 Bludgeoning damage when you cast the spell and at the end of each of your turns. If a structure reaches 0 Hit Points, it collapses.

A creature within half the structure’s height must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 12d6 Bludgeoning damage, falls Prone, and is buried in rubble, requiring a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check to escape. On a success, it takes half damage only.

Name: Eldritch Blast
Text: You fire a bolt of crackling energy. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature or object within range. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The spell creates two beams at level 5, three beams at level 11, and four beams at level 17. You can direct the beams at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each beam.

Name: Elemental Weapon
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: You touch a nonmagical weapon, imbuing it with magic. Choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. For the duration, the weapon gains a +1 bonus to attack rolls and deals an extra 1d4 damage of the chosen type on a hit.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you use a level 5-6 spell slot, the bonus to attack rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage increases to 2d4. If you use a level 7+ spell slot, the bonus increases to +3, and the extra damage increases to 3d4.

Name: Elementalism
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Elements (PHB'24) Monk, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You manipulate elemental forces to produce one of the following effects within range.

Beckon Air. A light breeze stirs within a 5-foot Cube, enough to flutter cloth, shift dust, rustle leaves, and close unsecured doors or shutters. Anything held open is unaffected.

Beckon Earth. You spread a thin layer of dust or sand over a 5-foot square, or you inscribe a single word of your choice in dirt or sand in your own handwriting.

Beckon Fire. You summon a harmless cloud of glowing embers and scented, colored smoke in a 5-foot Cube. You choose the color and scent. The embers can ignite candles, torches, or lamps. The smoke’s scent lingers for 1 minute.

Beckon Water. You create a cool mist that lightly dampens creatures and objects in a 5-foot Cube. Alternatively, you create 1 cup of clean water in an open container or on a surface; it evaporates after 1 minute.

Sculpt Element. You shape dirt, sand, fire, smoke, mist, or water that fits within a 1-foot Cube into a crude form—such as a simple creature shape—for 1 hour.

Name: Enhance Ability
Components: V, S, M (fur or a feather)
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You touch a creature and choose Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. For the duration, the target has Advantage on all ability checks using the chosen ability.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2. You can choose a different ability for each target.

Name: Enlarge/Reduce
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of powdered iron)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
For the duration, you enlarge or reduce a creature or object you can see within range (see below). A targeted object must not be worn or carried. An unwilling creature may make a Constitution saving throw to resist the effect.

Everything the creature wears or carries changes size with it. Items return to normal size when dropped. Thrown weapons or ammunition revert to normal size immediately after hitting or missing.

Enlarge. The target’s size increases by one category (for example, Medium to Large). It gains Advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and its attacks with enlarged weapons or Unarmed Strikes deal an extra 1d4 damage.

Reduce. The target’s size decreases by one category (for example, Medium to Small). It has Disadvantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and its attacks with reduced weapons or Unarmed Strikes deal 1d4 less damage (minimum 1).

Name: Ensnaring Strike
Subclasses: Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin
Text:
When you hit a creature, thorny vines wrap around it, and it must make a Strength saving throw. Large or larger creatures have Advantage on this save. On a failed save, the creature becomes Restrained until the spell ends. On a successful save, the vines wither and the spell ends.

While Restrained, the creature takes 1d6 Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. The creature or another creature within reach may use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the spell on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Entangle
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
Grasping plants erupt from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. For the duration, the area becomes Difficult Terrain.

Each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must make a Strength saving throw or become Restrained until the spell ends. A Restrained creature may use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, freeing itself on a success.

Name: Enthrall
Text: You weave distracting words that muddle perception. Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. Creatures you or your allies are fighting automatically succeed. On a failed save, the creature takes a –10 penalty to Wisdom (Perception) checks and to Passive Perception for the duration.

Name: Etherealness
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You step into the Border Ethereal, overlapping your current plane. You remain there for the duration and may move in any direction; moving vertically costs an extra foot of movement per foot traveled. You can see the plane you left in muted gray tones, but nothing beyond 60 feet.

While on the Ethereal Plane, you can affect and be affected only by creatures and effects also on that plane. Creatures not on the Ethereal Plane cannot perceive or interact with you unless they have a special ability that allows it.

When the spell ends, you return to the corresponding space on the plane you left. If that space is occupied, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take Force damage equal to twice the number of feet moved.

The spell ends immediately if cast while you are already on the Ethereal Plane or on a plane that does not border it, such as an Outer Plane.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target up to three willing creatures (including yourself) for each spell slot level above 7. The creatures must be within 10 feet of you when you cast the spell.

Name: Black Tentacles
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
Black, writhing tentacles fill a 20-foot square on the ground within range. For the duration, the area becomes Difficult Terrain.

Each creature in the area must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 3d6 Bludgeoning damage and becomes Restrained until the spell ends. A creature must also make this save if it enters the area or ends its turn there, but no more than once per turn.

A Restrained creature may use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the condition on a success.

Name: Expeditious Retreat
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You immediately take the Dash action, and until the spell ends, you may take the Dash action again as a Bonus Action on each of your turns.

Name: Eyebite
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
For the duration, your eyes become pools of darkness. One creature you can see within 60 feet must make a Wisdom saving throw or suffer one of the following effects of your choice:

On each of your turns, you may use a Magic action to target a different creature, but you cannot target a creature again if it has succeeded on a save against this casting.

Asleep. The creature becomes Unconscious. It wakes if it takes damage or if another creature uses an action to shake it awake.

Panicked. The creature becomes Frightened. On each of its turns, it must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest route. If it reaches a space at least 60 feet away where it cannot see you, the effect ends.

Sickened. The creature becomes Poisoned.

Name: Fabricate
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric
Text:
You reshape raw materials into finished goods of the same substance. For example, you might turn trees into a wooden bridge, hemp into rope, or flax or wool into clothing.

Choose raw materials you can see within range. Provided you have enough material, you can create a Large or smaller object (fitting within a 10-foot Cube or eight connected 5-foot Cubes). If you work with metal, stone, or other mineral substances, the finished object can be no larger than Medium (fitting within a 5-foot Cube). The quality of the result depends on the quality of the materials.

This spell cannot create creatures or magic items. You also cannot fabricate complex items—such as weapons or armor—unless you are proficient with the appropriate Artisan’s Tools.

Name: Faerie Fire
Subclasses: Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text:
Objects in a 20-foot Cube within range become outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Each creature in the area is also outlined if it fails a Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, outlined creatures and objects shed Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and cannot benefit from the Invisible condition.

Attack rolls against an outlined target have Advantage if the attacker can see it.

Name: False Life
Components: V, S, M (a drop of alcohol)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text: You gain 2d4 + 4 Temporary Hit Points.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You gain 5 additional Temporary Hit Points for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Fear
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Gloom Stalker (PHB'24) Ranger, Gloom Stalker (XGE) Ranger, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin
Text:
Each creature in a 30-foot Cone must make a Wisdom saving throw or drop whatever it is holding and become Frightened for the duration.

A Frightened creature must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest route on each of its turns unless it has nowhere to go. If it ends its turn in a space where it cannot see you, it makes another Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Feather Fall
Components: V, M (a small feather or piece of down)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: Choose up to five falling creatures within range. Each creature’s rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until the spell ends. If a creature reaches the ground before the spell ends, it takes no falling damage, and the spell ends for that creature.

Name: Feign Death
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of graveyard dirt)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text:
You touch a willing creature and place it into a deathlike trance.

For the duration, the target appears dead to observers and to spells that detect life or status. The target is Blinded, Incapacitated, and has a Speed of 0.

The target also gains Resistance to all damage except Psychic damage and becomes Immune to the Poisoned condition.

Name: Find Familiar
Components: V, S, M (burning incense worth 10+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You summon a familiar—a spirit that takes the form of a Bat, Cat, Frog, Hawk, Lizard, Octopus, Owl, Rat, Raven, Spider, Weasel, or another CR 0 Beast. It appears in an unoccupied space within range and uses the statistics of its chosen form, but it is a Celestial, Fey, or Fiend (your choice).

Telepathic Connection. While the familiar is within 100 feet of you, you can communicate with it telepathically. As a Bonus Action, you can see through its eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn, gaining any special senses it has.

Delivering Spells. When you cast a spell with a range of touch, your familiar can deliver the spell for you. It must be within 100 feet of you and use its Reaction to deliver the spell.

Combat. The familiar is an ally to you and your allies. It rolls its own Initiative and acts independently. It cannot attack but can take other actions.

Disappearance. If the familiar drops to 0 Hit Points, it disappears. It reappears when you cast this spell again. As a Magic action, you may temporarily dismiss it to a pocket dimension or dismiss it permanently. While dismissed, you may use a Magic action to return it to an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you. Anything it was wearing or carrying is left behind.

One Familiar Only. You cannot have more than one familiar at a time. Casting this spell again changes the familiar’s form instead of creating a new one.

Name: Find Steed
Text:
You summon a supernatural steed that appears in an unoccupied space within range, using the Otherworldly Steed stat block. If you already have a steed from this spell, it is replaced.

The steed resembles a Large mount of your choice—horse, camel, dire wolf, elk, etc. When you cast the spell, choose whether the steed is a Celestial, Fey, or Fiend; this determines certain traits.

Combat. The steed is an ally to you and your allies. It shares your Initiative count and functions as a controlled mount while you ride it. If you become Incapacitated, it acts independently on its turn, prioritizing your safety.

Disappearance. The steed vanishes if it drops to 0 Hit Points or if you die, leaving behind anything it carried. When you cast this spell again, you may summon the same steed or a different one.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

Name: Find Traps
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You sense any trap within range that is within line of sight. A trap is any object or mechanism designed to cause harm or danger. This includes magical traps such as Alarm or Glyph of Warding, as well as mechanical traps like pit traps. It does not detect natural hazards such as unstable floors or sinkholes.

The spell reveals only that a trap exists, not its exact location. You do learn the general nature of the danger it poses.

Name: Find the Path
Components: V, S, M (a set of divination tools—such as cards or runes—worth 100+ GP)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You magically determine the most direct physical route to a location you name. You must be familiar with the destination. The spell fails if the destination is on another plane, is moving (such as a mobile fortress), or is too vague (such as “a green dragon’s lair”).

For the duration, as long as you remain on the same plane as the destination, you know its distance and direction. Whenever you face a choice of paths, you know which route is the most direct.

Name: Finger of Death
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You release a surge of necrotic power toward a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 7d8 + 30 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

If this spell kills a Humanoid, it rises at the start of your next turn as a Zombie under your command, obeying your verbal orders.

Name: Fire Bolt
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Arid Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You fling a small burst of fire at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Fire damage. A flammable object struck by the spell ignites if it is not being worn or carried.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

Name: Fire Shield
Components: V, S, M (a bit of phosphorus or a firefly)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, War (PHB'24) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Flickering flames surround your body for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for another 10 feet.

You choose whether the flames form a warm shield or a chill shield. A warm shield grants Resistance to Cold damage; a chill shield grants Resistance to Fire damage.

Additionally, whenever a creature within 5 feet hits you with a melee attack, the shield lashes out: a warm shield deals 2d8 Fire damage to the attacker, and a chill shield deals 2d8 Cold damage.

Name: Fire Storm
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You summon a storm of fire in an area within range. The storm consists of up to ten 10-foot Cubes arranged however you choose, as long as each Cube touches at least one other. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Unattended flammable objects in the area ignite.

Name: Fireball
Components: V, S, M (a ball of bat guano and sulfur)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Land (Arid Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A streak of flame arcs from you to a point within range and erupts into a fiery explosion. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Flammable objects not being worn or carried ignite.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Flame Blade
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
You conjure a blade of fire in your free hand. It resembles a scimitar and lasts for the duration. If you release the blade, it vanishes, but you may recreate it as a Bonus Action.

As a Magic action, you may make a melee spell attack with the blade. On a hit, the target takes 3d6 + your spellcasting ability modifier Fire damage.

The blade sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Flame Strike
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of sulfur)
Subclasses: Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, War (PHB'14) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: A pillar of blazing fire descends from above. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d6 Fire damage and 5d6 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Fire damage and the Radiant damage increase by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Flaming Sphere
Components: V, S, M (a ball of wax)
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text:
You create a 5-foot-diameter sphere of fire in an unoccupied space on the ground within range. It remains for the duration. Any creature ending its turn within 5 feet of the sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

As a Bonus Action, you may move the sphere up to 30 feet, rolling it along the ground. If you move it into a creature’s space, that creature makes the save, and the sphere stops moving.

You may direct the sphere over obstacles up to 5 feet tall and across gaps up to 10 feet wide. Flammable objects not being worn or carried ignite on contact. The sphere sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for another 20 feet.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Flesh to Stone
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You attempt to turn a creature you can see within range to stone. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Restrained for the duration. On a successful save, its Speed becomes 0 until the start of your next turn. Constructs automatically succeed.

A Restrained creature repeats the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. Three successful saves end the spell; three failed saves turn the creature to stone, giving it the Petrified condition for the duration. Track successes and failures separately.

If you maintain Concentration for the full duration, the Petrification becomes permanent until ended by Greater Restoration or similar magic.

Name: Fly
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin
Text: You touch a willing creature, granting it a Fly Speed of 60 feet and the ability to hover for the duration. When the spell ends, the creature falls if it is still airborne and cannot prevent the fall.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Fog Cloud
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock
Text: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The area is Heavily Obscured. The fog lasts for the duration or until a strong wind—such as one produced by Gust of Wind—disperses it.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The fog's radius increases by 20 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Forbiddance
Components: V, S, M (ruby dust worth 1,000+ GP)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You ward an area of up to 40,000 square feet of floor space, extending 30 feet upward. For the duration, creatures cannot teleport into the area or enter it through portals such as those created by Gate. The area is shielded from planar travel, preventing entry via the Astral Plane, Ethereal Plane, Feywild, Shadowfell, or Plane Shift.

When you cast the spell, choose any number of creature types from Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead. A creature of a chosen type that enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there takes 5d10 Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice when casting).

You may set a password when casting the spell. A creature that speaks the password upon entering takes no damage.

The area cannot overlap with another Forbiddance spell. If you cast Forbiddance on the same location every day for 30 days, the effect becomes permanent until dispelled, and the material components are consumed on the final casting.

Name: Forcecage
Components: V, S, M (ruby dust worth 1,500+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create an immobile, Invisible Cube of magical force in an area within range. The prison may be a cage or a solid box.

A cage can be up to 20 feet on a side, formed from half-inch bars spaced half an inch apart. A solid box can be up to 10 feet on a side, completely blocking matter and spells from passing through.

Creatures fully inside the area when the spell is cast are trapped. Creatures partially inside or too large are pushed outward until fully outside.

A creature inside cannot leave by nonmagical means. To escape via teleportation or planar travel, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the attempt is wasted. The cage extends into the Ethereal Plane, blocking ethereal movement.

Dispel Magic cannot end this spell.

Name: Foresight
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You touch a willing creature, granting it a glimpse of the immediate future. For the duration, the target has Advantage on all d20 Tests, and attack rolls made against it have Disadvantage. The spell ends early if you cast it again.

Name: Fount of Moonlight
Subclasses: Moon (PHB'24) Druid
Text:
A cool radiance surrounds you, shedding Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for another 20 feet.

You gain Resistance to Radiant damage, and your melee attacks deal an extra 2d6 Radiant damage.

Additionally, when a creature you can see within 60 feet damages you, you may use your Reaction to force it to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes Blinded until the end of your next turn.

Name: Freedom of Movement
Subclasses: Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Temperate Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, War (PHB'14) Cleric, War (PHB'24) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You touch a willing creature. For the duration, its movement is unaffected by Difficult Terrain, and magical effects cannot reduce its Speed or impose the Paralyzed or Restrained conditions. The creature also gains a Swim Speed equal to its Speed.

Additionally, it may spend 5 feet of movement to automatically escape nonmagical restraints, including manacles or a creature Grappling it.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Friends
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You project magical friendliness toward a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. It automatically succeeds if it is not a Humanoid, if you are fighting it, or if you cast this spell on it within the last 24 hours.

The spell ends early if the target takes damage, or if you make an attack roll, deal damage, or force a saving throw. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Name: Gaseous Form
Components: V, S, M (a bit of gauze)
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger
Text:
You transform a willing creature—and everything it is wearing and carrying—into a cloud of mist for the duration. The spell ends early if the target drops to 0 Hit Points or uses a Magic action to end the effect.

In this form, the target has a Fly Speed of 10 feet and can hover. It can enter another creature’s space, has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, is Immune to the Prone condition, and has Advantage on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws. It can pass through narrow openings but treats liquids as solid.

The target cannot speak, manipulate objects, drop items, use items, attack, or cast spells.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Gate
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 5,000+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You open a portal between an unoccupied space you can see within range and a specific location on another plane. The portal is a circular opening 5 to 20 feet in diameter, oriented however you choose. It lasts for the duration, and the destination is visible through it.

The portal has a front and back on each plane. Only creatures and objects entering through the front are transported, appearing in the nearest unoccupied space on the other side.

Deities and planar rulers may prevent portals from opening in their presence or domains.

When casting the spell, you may speak the true name of a specific creature. If that creature is on another plane, the portal opens next to it and pulls it through to the nearest unoccupied space on your side. You gain no control over the creature once it arrives.

Name: Geas
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin
Text:
You speak a command to a creature you can see within range, compelling it to perform or avoid an action you choose. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. It automatically succeeds if it cannot understand your command.

While Charmed, if the creature directly disobeys your command, it takes 5d10 Psychic damage (no more than once per day).

You may issue any command that does not guarantee the creature’s death. If you give a suicidal order, the spell ends.

Remove Curse, Greater Restoration, or Wish ends this spell.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. If you use a level 7 or 8 spell slot, the duration is 365 days. If you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until it is ended by one of the spells mentioned above.

Name: Gentle Repose
Components: V, S, M (2 Copper Pieces, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Spores (TCE) Druid, Spores (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric
Text:
You touch a corpse or other remains. For the duration, the target is protected from decay and cannot become Undead.

Time spent under this spell does not count against the time limit for spells that return a creature to life, such as Raise Dead.

Name: Giant Insect
Text:
You summon a giant centipede, spider, or wasp (your choice when casting). It appears in an unoccupied space you can see within range and uses the Giant Insect stat block. The chosen form determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. It shares your Initiative count but takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required). If you give no commands, it takes the Dodge action and moves to avoid harm.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

Name: Glibness
Text: Until the spell ends, whenever you make a Charisma check, you may replace the number rolled with a 15. Additionally, any magic used to determine whether you are telling the truth indicates that you are being truthful, regardless of what you say.

Name: Globe of Invulnerability
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
A shimmering, immobile barrier forms in a 10-foot Emanation around you and lasts for the duration.

Any spell of level 5 or lower cast from outside the barrier cannot affect anything inside it. Such spells may target creatures or objects within the barrier, but they have no effect. Likewise, the barrier’s interior is excluded from areas of effect created by such spells.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The barrier blocks spells of 1 level higher for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Glyph of Warding
Components: V, S, M (powdered diamond worth 200+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You inscribe a glyph that later unleashes magical energy. You inscribe it on a surface (such as a floor or table) or inside a closable object (such as a book or chest). The glyph can cover an area up to 10 feet in diameter. If the surface or object is moved more than 10 feet from where you cast the spell, the glyph breaks and the spell ends without triggering.

The glyph is nearly invisible and requires a successful Wisdom (Perception) check against your spell save DC to detect.

When inscribing the glyph, you set its trigger and choose whether it is an explosive rune or a spell glyph.

Trigger. You decide what activates the glyph. Common triggers include touching or stepping on it, removing a covering object, opening the container it is inscribed in, or approaching within a set distance. You may refine the trigger to affect only certain creature types or to ignore creatures that meet conditions such as speaking a password.

Explosive Rune. When triggered, the glyph erupts in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (chosen when you create the glyph) on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Spell Glyph. You may store a prepared spell of level 3 or lower in the glyph by casting it as part of creating the glyph. The stored spell has no immediate effect. When the glyph triggers, the stored spell activates. If the spell targets a creature, it targets the creature that triggered the glyph. If it affects an area, the area is centered on that creature. Hostile creatures or harmful effects created by the spell appear as close as possible to the intruder. If the stored spell requires Concentration, it lasts for its full duration.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage of an explosive rune increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3. If you create a spell glyph, you can store any spell of up to the same level as the spell slot you use for the Glyph of Warding.

Name: Goodberry
Components: V, S, M (a sprig of mistletoe)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
Ten magical berries appear in your hand and last for the duration. A creature may use a Bonus Action to eat one berry. Eating a berry restores 1 Hit Point and provides enough nourishment for one day.

Uneaten berries vanish when the spell ends.

Name: Grasping Vine
Subclasses: Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You conjure a vine that sprouts from a surface in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The vine lasts for the duration.

Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 30 feet of the vine. On a hit, the target takes 4d8 Bludgeoning damage and is pulled up to 30 feet toward the vine. If the target is Huge or smaller, it becomes Grappled (escape DC equals your spell save DC). The vine can grapple only one creature at a time, and you may release the creature at any time (no action required).

As a Bonus Action on later turns, you may repeat the attack against a creature within 30 feet of the vine.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The number of creatures the vine can grapple increases by one for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Grease
Components: V, S, M (a bit of pork rind or butter)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
Nonflammable grease coats the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within range, turning the area into Difficult Terrain for the duration.

When the grease appears, each creature standing in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall Prone. A creature entering the area or ending its turn there must also succeed on the save or fall Prone.

Name: Greater Invisibility
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Gloom Stalker (XGE) Ranger, Gloom Stalker (PHB'24) Ranger, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text: You touch a creature, granting it the Invisible condition for the duration.

Name: Greater Restoration
Components: V, S, M (diamond dust worth 100+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text:
You touch a creature and remove one of the following effects:

• 1 level of Exhaustion
• The Charmed or Petrified condition
• A curse, including Attunement to a cursed magic item
• Any reduction to one of the creature’s ability scores
• Any reduction to the creature’s Hit Point maximum

Name: Guardian of Faith
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Celestial (XGE) Warlock, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A Large spectral guardian appears and hovers for the duration in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The guardian occupies that space, is invulnerable, and takes a form appropriate to your deity or pantheon.

Any enemy that enters a space within 10 feet of the guardian for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 20 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. The guardian disappears once it has dealt a total of 60 damage.

Name: Guards and Wards
Components: V, S, M (a silver rod worth 10+ GP)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You create a ward protecting up to 2,500 square feet of floor space, up to 20 feet high. You may shape the ward as one 50-foot square, one hundred contiguous 5-foot squares, or twenty-five contiguous 10-foot squares.

When casting the spell, you may designate individuals who are unaffected, and you may set a password that grants immunity to the ward’s effects when spoken within 5 feet of the area.

The ward produces the following effects. Dispel Magic cannot end Guards and Wards itself, but each individual effect can be dispelled. If all four are dispelled, the spell ends. Casting this spell daily for 365 days on the same area makes it permanent until all effects are dispelled.

Corridors. Fog fills all warded corridors, making them Heavily Obscured. At each intersection or branching path, there is a 50% chance that a creature other than you believes it is moving in the opposite direction.

Doors. All doors in the area are magically locked as if by Arcane Lock. You may also disguise up to ten doors with illusions to appear as blank walls.

Stairs. Webs fill all stairs in the area, as in the Web spell. Destroyed webs regrow in 10 minutes.

Other Spell Effect. Choose one of the following to place within the warded area:
• Dancing Lights in four corridors, following a simple repeating pattern.
• Magic Mouth in two locations.
• Stinking Cloud in two locations (vapors return in 10 minutes if dispersed).
• Gust of Wind in one corridor or room (continuous).
• Suggestion in one 5-foot square; any creature entering receives the suggestion mentally.

Name: Guidance
Subclasses: Stars (TCE) Druid, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Stars (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Spirits (VRGR) Bard, Spirits (VRGR) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You touch a willing creature and choose a skill. For the duration, the creature adds 1d4 to any ability check using that skill.

Name: Guiding Bolt
Subclasses: Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Stars (TCE) Druid, Stars (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock, War (PHB'24) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You hurl a bolt of radiant light at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 Radiant damage, and the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn has Advantage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Gust of Wind
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
A 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide Line of strong wind blasts from you in a direction you choose for the duration. Each creature in the Line must make a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you along the Line. A creature that ends its turn in the Line must repeat the save.

A creature moving closer to you through the Line must spend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot moved.

The wind disperses gas and vapor, extinguishes candles and similar unprotected flames, and causes protected flames to flicker wildly with a 50% chance of extinguishing them.

As a Bonus Action on later turns, you may change the direction of the Line.

Name: Hail of Thorns
Text: When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack, this spell causes a burst of thorns to erupt from the weapon or ammunition. The target and each creature within 5 feet of it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 1d10 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Hallow
Components: V, S, M (incense worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You touch a point and infuse the surrounding area with holy or unholy power. The area may have a radius up to 60 feet. The spell fails if the radius overlaps an existing Hallow effect. The area gains the following effects:

Hallowed Ward. Choose any number of the following creature types: Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, Undead. Creatures of the chosen types cannot willingly enter the area. Creatures possessed by or Charmed or Frightened by such creatures are freed of those effects while in the area.

Extra Effect. Choose one of the following to bind to the area:
• Courage: Chosen creature types cannot gain the Frightened condition.
• Darkness: The area is filled with Darkness. Normal light and magical light of lower level cannot illuminate it.
• Daylight: The area is filled with Bright Light. Magical Darkness of lower level cannot extinguish it.
• Peaceful Rest: Corpses in the area cannot become Undead.
• Extradimensional Interference: Chosen creature types cannot teleport or use interplanar travel into or out of the area.
• Fear: Chosen creature types have the Frightened condition.
• Resistance: Chosen creature types gain Resistance to one damage type you choose.
• Silence: No sound can enter or leave the area.
• Tongues: Chosen creature types can communicate with any creature in the area.
• Vulnerability: Chosen creature types gain Vulnerability to one damage type you choose.

Name: Hallucinatory Terrain
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer
Text:
You alter natural terrain within a 150-foot Cube to look, sound, and smell like another type of natural terrain. Open fields may appear as swamps, cliffs as gentle slopes, ponds as grassy meadows, etc. Manufactured structures, equipment, and creatures are unchanged.

The illusion does not alter tactile sensations, so creatures entering the area may notice inconsistencies. A creature may take the Study action to make an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to recognize the illusion. A creature that succeeds sees a faint overlay of the illusion atop the real terrain.

Name: Harm
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: You unleash virulent magic on a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 14d6 Necrotic damage, and its Hit Point maximum is reduced by the same amount. On a successful save, it takes half damage only. This spell cannot reduce a creature’s Hit Point maximum below 1.

Name: Haste
Components: V, S, M (a shaving of licorice root)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Choose a willing creature you can see within range. Until the spell ends, the target’s Speed is doubled, it gains a +2 bonus to Armor Class, has Advantage on Dexterity saving throws, and gains an additional action on each of its turns. That action may be used only to take the Attack (one attack only), Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Utilize action.

When the spell ends, the target becomes Incapacitated and its Speed becomes 0 until the end of its next turn, as lethargy overtakes it.

Name: Heal
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: Choose a creature you can see within range. Positive energy floods the target, restoring 70 Hit Points. The spell also ends the Blinded, Deafened, and Poisoned conditions on the target.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 10 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Healing Word
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: A creature of your choice that you can see within range regains Hit Points equal to 2d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Heat Metal
Components: V, S, M (a piece of iron and a flame)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric
Text:
Choose a manufactured metal object you can see within range—such as a metal weapon or a suit of Medium or Heavy metal armor. The object glows red-hot. Any creature in physical contact with the object takes 2d8 Fire damage when you cast the spell. Until the spell ends, you may use a Bonus Action on each of your turns to deal this damage again if the object is within range.

If a creature is holding or wearing the object and takes this damage, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or drop the object if possible. If it does not drop it, it has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks until the start of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Hellish Rebuke
Subclasses: Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin
Text: The creature that damaged you becomes briefly surrounded by green flames. It must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d10 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Heroes' Feast
Components: V, S, M (a gem-encrusted bowl worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You conjure a magnificent feast that appears on a surface within an unoccupied 10-foot Cube next to you. The feast takes 1 hour to consume and disappears afterward. Up to twelve creatures may partake, and the benefits begin only after the hour is complete.

A creature that partakes gains the following benefits for 24 hours:
• Resistance to Poison damage
• Immunity to the Frightened and Poisoned conditions
• Its Hit Point maximum increases by 2d10, and it gains the same number of Hit Points

Name: Heroism
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric
Text: You touch a willing creature and instill supernatural bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is Immune to the Frightened condition and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Hex
Components: V, S, M (the petrified eye of a newt)
Subclasses: Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock
Text:
You place a curse on a creature you can see within range. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. Also choose one ability when you cast the spell; the target has Disadvantage on ability checks using that ability.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, you may use a Bonus Action on a later turn to transfer the curse to a new creature.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 2 (up to 4 hours), 3-4 (up to 8 hours), or 5+ (24 hours).

Name: Hold Monster
Components: V, S, M (a straight piece of iron)
Subclasses: Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, War (PHB'14) Cleric, War (PHB'24) Cleric
Text: Choose a creature you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Paralyzed for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Hold Person
Components: V, S, M (a straight piece of iron)
Classes: Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric, Winter Walker (FRHoF) Ranger
Text: Choose a Humanoid you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Paralyzed for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional Humanoid for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Holy Aura
Components: V, S, M (a reliquary worth 1,000+ GP)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
For the duration, you emit an aura in a 30-foot Emanation. While in the aura, creatures of your choice have Advantage on all saving throws, and other creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against them.

Additionally, when a Fiend or Undead hits an affected creature with a melee attack, the attacker must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become Blinded until the end of its next turn.

Name: Hunger of Hadarc
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock
Text:
You tear open a gateway to the Far Realm. A 20-foot-radius Sphere of Darkness appears, centered on a point within range, lasting for the duration. The Sphere is Difficult Terrain and filled with unsettling whispers and slurping sounds audible up to 30 feet away. No light—magical or nonmagical—can illuminate it, and creatures fully inside are Blinded.

Any creature that starts its turn in the area takes 2d6 Cold damage. Any creature that ends its turn there must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 Acid damage from writhing otherworldly tentacles.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Cold or Acid damage (your choice) increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Hunter's Mark
Subclasses: Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin
Text:
You magically mark a creature you can see within range as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Force damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. You also have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception or Survival) checks made to find it.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before the spell ends, you may use a Bonus Action to move the mark to a new creature you can see within range.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 3-4 (up to 8 hours) or 5+ (up to 24 hours).

Name: Hypnotic Pattern
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric
Text:
You create a swirling pattern of hypnotic colors in a 30-foot Cube within range. The pattern appears briefly and vanishes. Each creature in the area that can see it must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration. While Charmed, the creature is Incapacitated and its Speed is 0.

The spell ends for an affected creature if it takes damage or if another creature uses an action to shake it out of its stupor.

Name: Ice Knife
Components: S, M (a drop of water or a piece of ice)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Winter Walker (FRHoF) Ranger
Text: You form a shard of ice and hurl it at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Piercing damage. Hit or miss, the shard then explodes. The target and each creature within 5 feet must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 Cold damage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Cold damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Ice Storm
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Winter Walker (FRHoF) Ranger
Text:
Hailstones batter a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d10 Bludgeoning damage and 4d6 Cold damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

The hail turns the ground in the area into Difficult Terrain until the end of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Bludgeoning damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Identify
Components: V, S, M (a pearl worth 100+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You touch an object for the duration of the casting. If the object is a magic item or another magical object, you learn its properties, how to use it, whether it requires Attunement, and how many charges it has (if any). You also learn what spells, if any, are affecting it, and whether it was created by a spell—and if so, which spell.

If you instead touch a creature, you learn which spells, if any, are currently affecting it.

Name: Illusory Script
Components: S, M (ink worth 10+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You write on parchment, paper, or similar material and imbue the writing with an illusion for the duration. To you and creatures you designate, the writing appears normal and conveys the intended meaning. To all others, it appears as unintelligible magical script. Alternatively, the illusion may alter the text’s meaning, handwriting, or language, provided the language is one you know.

If the spell is dispelled, both the original and illusory text vanish.

A creature with Truesight can read the hidden message.

Name: Imprisonment
Components: V, S, M (a statuette of the target worth 5,000+ GP)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You create a magical prison to restrain a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a success, it is unaffected and becomes immune to this spell for 24 hours. On a failure, it is imprisoned. While imprisoned, the creature does not need to breathe, eat, or drink, does not age, cannot teleport, and cannot be detected by Divination magic.

Choose one of the following forms of imprisonment:

Burial. The target is entombed underground in a hollow sphere of magical force just large enough to contain it. Nothing can pass through the sphere.

Chaining. Chains anchored to the ground hold the target immobile. It is Restrained and cannot be moved by any means.

Hedged Prison. The target is confined in a demiplane warded against teleportation and planar travel. The demiplane may resemble a labyrinth, cage, tower, or similar structure.

Minimus Containment. The target shrinks to 1 inch tall and is trapped inside an indestructible gemstone or similar object. Light passes through, but nothing else.

Slumber. The target falls into an Unconscious state and cannot be awakened.

Ending the Spell. When casting, you specify a trigger that will end the spell. The trigger must be an observable event with a high likelihood of occurring within the next decade. Examples include a specific offering being made, a loved one being saved, or a particular monster being defeated.

Only Dispel Magic cast with a 9th-level spell slot can end this spell, targeting either the prison or the material component.

Name: Incendiary Cloud
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
A swirling cloud of embers and smoke fills a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range. The area is Heavily Obscured. The cloud lasts for the duration or until dispersed by strong wind (such as Gust of Wind).

When the cloud appears, each creature inside must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 Fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature must also make this save when the Sphere moves into its space, when it enters the Sphere, or when it ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

At the start of each of your turns, the cloud moves 10 feet away from you in a direction you choose.

Name: Inflict Wounds
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin, Oathbreaker (DMG'14) Paladin
Text: You touch a creature, which must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 2d10 Necrotic damage; on a successful save, it takes half as much.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Insect Plague
Subclasses: Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Swarming locusts fill a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range. The Sphere remains for the duration, and its area is Lightly Obscured and Difficult Terrain.

When the swarm appears, each creature in it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 4d10 Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature must also make this save when it enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Invisibility
Components: V, S, M (an eyelash in gum arabic)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric
Text: A creature you touch becomes Invisible until the spell ends. The spell ends early immediately after the target makes an attack roll, deals damage, or casts a spell.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Storm of Radiance
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of phosphorus)
Subclasses: Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You unleash a storm of flashing light and thunder within a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point you can see within range. Creatures in the area are Blinded and Deafened and cannot cast spells with a Verbal component.

When the storm appears, each creature in it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d10 Radiant damage and 2d10 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature must also make this save when it enters the area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The Radiant and Thunder damage increase by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Jump
Components: V, S, M (a grasshopper's hind leg)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You touch a willing creature. Once on each of its turns until the spell ends, the creature may jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Knock
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
Choose an object you can see within range—such as a door, chest, box, set of manacles, padlock, or any object secured by mundane or magical means.

If the object is held shut by a mundane lock, stuck, or barred, it becomes unlocked, unstuck, or unbarred. If it has multiple locks, only one is unlocked.

If the object is secured by Arcane Lock, that spell is suppressed for 10 minutes, during which the object may be opened and closed.

When you cast the spell, a loud knock audible up to 300 feet emanates from the target.

Name: Legend Lore
Components: V, S, M (incense worth 250+ GP, which the spell consumes, and four ivory strips worth 50+ GP each)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Draconic (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text:
Name or describe a famous person, place, or object. The spell brings to your mind a brief summary of significant lore about the subject, as determined by the DM.

The lore may include important facts, surprising details, or even secret knowledge. The more you already know, the more precise the information. The information is accurate but may be delivered in figurative or poetic language.

If the subject is not actually famous, you hear a sad trombone sound and the spell fails.

Name: Secret Chest
Components: V, S, M (a chest, 3 feet by 2 feet by 2 feet, constructed from rare materials worth 5,000+ GP, and a Tiny replica of the chest made from the same materials worth 50+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter
Text:
You hide a chest and its contents on the Ethereal Plane. You must touch both the chest and the miniature replica used as Material components. The chest may contain up to 12 cubic feet of nonliving material.

While the chest remains on the Ethereal Plane, you may take a Magic action and touch the replica to recall it, causing it to appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You may send it back by taking a Magic action and touching both chest and replica.

After 60 days, there is a cumulative 5% chance each day that the spell ends. It also ends if you cast this spell again or if the replica is destroyed. If the spell ends while the chest is on the Ethereal Plane, it remains there.

Name: Tiny Hut
