Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text:
A 10-foot Emanation appears around you and remains stationary for the duration. The spell fails if the Emanation is not large enough to fully contain all creatures within it when cast.

Creatures and objects inside the Emanation when you cast the spell may move through it freely. All others are barred from passing through. Spells of level 3 or lower cannot be cast through it, and their effects cannot extend into it.

The interior remains comfortable and dry regardless of outside weather. You may command the interior to be Dim Light or Darkness (no action required). The Emanation is opaque from outside in any color you choose, but transparent from within.

The spell ends early if you leave the Emanation or cast it again.

Name: Lesser Restoration
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text: You touch a creature and end one of the following conditions affecting it: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

Name: Levitate
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
One creature or loose object you can see within range rises vertically up to 20 feet and remains suspended for the duration. The spell can levitate an object weighing up to 500 pounds. An unwilling creature that succeeds on a Constitution saving throw is unaffected.

The target may move by pushing or pulling against a fixed surface, moving as if climbing. You may change its altitude by up to 20 feet on your turn. If you are the target, you may move up or down as part of your movement; otherwise, you must take a Magic action to move the target, which must remain within range.

When the spell ends, the target floats gently to the ground if still aloft.

Covering the object with something opaque blocks the light. The spell ends if you cast it again.

Name: Lightning Arrow
Text:
As your attack hits or misses, the weapon or ammunition transforms into a bolt of lightning. Instead of the normal damage or effects of the attack, the target takes 4d8 Lightning damage on a hit or half as much on a miss. Each creature within 10 feet of the target must then make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

The weapon or ammunition then returns to normal.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage for both effects of the spell increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

Text: A stroke of lightning forms a 100-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line blasting from you in a direction you choose. Each creature in the Line must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Locate Animals or Plants
Components: V, S, M (fur from a bloodhound)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text: Describe or name a specific kind of Beast, Plant creature, or nonmagical plant. You learn the direction and distance to the nearest creature or plant of that kind within 5 miles, if any exist.

Classes: Bard (PHB'24), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Paladin (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)

Text:

You may locate a specific creature you know or the nearest creature of a specific kind (such as a human or unicorn) if you have seen such a creature up close—within 30 feet—at least once. If the creature is in a different form (such as via Flesh to Stone or Polymorph), the spell cannot locate it.

Any thickness of lead blocks the spell.

Text:
Describe or name an object familiar to you. You sense the direction to its location if it is within 1,000 feet. If it is moving, you know the direction of its movement.

Any thickness of lead blocks the spell.

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter

Text: You touch a willing creature not wearing armor. Until the spell ends, its base AC becomes 13 + its Dexterity modifier. The spell ends early if the target dons armor.

Name: Mage Hand
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger
Text:
A spectral hand appears at a point you choose within range and lasts for the duration. It disappears if it moves more than 30 feet from you or if you cast the spell again.

When you cast the spell, you may use the hand to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour out a vial.

As a Magic action on later turns, you may control the hand again and move it up to 30 feet.

The hand cannot attack, activate magic items, or carry more than 10 pounds.

Name: Magic Circle
Components: V, S, M (salt and powdered silver worth 100+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger
Text:
You create a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall Cylinder of magical energy centered on a point on the ground you can see. Glowing runes appear where the Cylinder meets surfaces.

Choose one or more creature types: Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, or Undead. The circle affects creatures of the chosen types as follows:

• They cannot willingly enter the Cylinder by nonmagical means. If they attempt to enter via teleportation or planar travel, they must succeed on a Charisma saving throw.
• They have Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets inside the Cylinder.
• Targets inside the Cylinder cannot be possessed by or gain the Charmed or Frightened condition from such creatures.

When casting the spell, you may reverse its function, preventing creatures of the chosen types from leaving the Cylinder and protecting creatures outside it.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The duration increases by 1 hour for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Magic Jar
Components: V, S, M (a gem, crystal, or reliquary worth 500+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
Your body becomes catatonic as your soul leaves it and enters the container used as the spell’s Material component. While your soul inhabits the container, you perceive your surroundings as though you occupied its space. You cannot move or take Reactions. The only action you can take is to project your soul up to 100 feet from the container, either returning to your body (ending the spell) or attempting to possess a Humanoid.

You may attempt to possess any Humanoid within 100 feet that you can see (creatures protected by Protection from Evil and Good or Magic Circle cannot be possessed). The target makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, your soul enters the target’s body, and the target’s soul becomes trapped in the container. On a successful save, the target resists, and you cannot attempt to possess it again for 24 hours.

While possessing a body, you control it. Your Hit Points, Hit Dice, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Speed, and senses are replaced by the host’s. You retain all other game statistics.

The host’s soul perceives from the container using its own senses but cannot move and is Incapacitated.

While possessing a body, you may take a Magic action to return to the container if it is within 100 feet, restoring the host’s soul to its body. If the host body dies while you inhabit it, the creature dies, and you must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a success, you return to the container if it is within 100 feet; otherwise, you die.

If the container is destroyed or the spell ends, your soul returns to your body. If your body is more than 100 feet away or is dead, you die. If another creature’s soul is in the container when it is destroyed, that soul returns to its body if alive and within 100 feet; otherwise, that creature dies.

When the spell ends, the container is destroyed.

Name: Magic Missile
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You create three glowing darts of magical force. Each dart hits a creature you can see within range. A dart deals 1d4 + 1 Force damage. The darts strike simultaneously, and you may direct them at one or multiple creatures.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The spell creates one more dart for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Magic Mouth

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You implant a message into an object you can see within range that is not worn or carried. You speak a message of up to 25 words, which may be delivered over up to 10 minutes, and define a trigger condition.

When the trigger occurs, a magical mouth appears on the object and speaks the message in your voice and volume. If the object has a mouth-like feature, the magical mouth appears there. You may choose for the spell to end after delivering the message or to repeat it whenever the trigger occurs.

The trigger must be based on visible or audible conditions occurring within 30 feet of the object.

At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The bonus increases to +2 with a level 3-5 spell slot. The bonus increases to +3 with a level 6+ spell slot.

Name: Major Image
Components: V, S, M (a bit of fleece)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create the image of an object, creature, or visible phenomenon no larger than a 20-foot Cube. The image appears at a point you can see within range and lasts for the duration. It appears real, including appropriate sounds, smells, and temperature, but cannot deal damage or impose conditions.

If you are within range, you may take a Magic action to move the image to another point within range. As it moves, you may alter its appearance to make the movement natural. You may also change its sounds.

Physical interaction reveals the illusion, as objects pass through it. A creature may take the Study action to examine the image and, on a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC, recognize it as an illusion. A creature that discerns the illusion sees through it, and its sensory qualities become faint.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The spell lasts until dispelled, without requiring Concentration, if cast with a level 4+ spell slot.

Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Moon (PHB'24) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: A wave of healing energy radiates from a point you can see within range. Choose up to six creatures in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point. Each target regains Hit Points equal to 5d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 5.

Name: Mass Heal
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: A surge of healing energy flows from you to creatures you can see within range. You restore up to 700 Hit Points, divided as you choose among any number of creatures. Creatures healed by this spell also have the Blinded, Deafened, and Poisoned conditions removed.

Name: Mass Healing Word
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text: Up to six creatures of your choice that you can see within range regain Hit Points equal to 2d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 1d4 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Mass Suggestion
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You suggest a course of activity—described in 25 words or fewer—to up to twelve creatures you can see within range that can hear and understand you. The suggestion must seem reasonable and cannot involve actions that would obviously harm the targets or their allies.

Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration or until you or your allies deal damage to it. A Charmed target follows the suggestion to the best of its ability. If the suggested activity can be completed sooner than the spell’s duration, the spell ends for that target once it completes the task.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The duration is longer with a spell slot of level 7 (10 days), 8 (30 days), or 9 (366 days).

Name: Maze
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You banish a creature you can see within range to a labyrinthine demiplane. The target remains there for the duration or until it escapes.

The target may take the Study action to attempt escape, making a DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check. On a success, it escapes and the spell ends.

When the spell ends, the target reappears in the space it left or, if that space is occupied, in the nearest unoccupied space.

Name: Meld into Stone
Subclasses: Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You step into a stone object or surface large enough to contain your body, merging yourself and your equipment with it for the duration. You must touch the stone to enter it. No trace of your presence is visible or detectable by nonmagical senses.

While merged, you cannot see outside the stone, and any Wisdom (Perception) checks to hear sounds outside are made with Disadvantage. You remain aware of time and may cast spells on yourself. You may use 5 feet of movement to exit the stone at the point where you entered, ending the spell. You cannot otherwise move.

Minor damage to the stone does not harm you, but partial destruction or reshaping that prevents you from fitting inside expels you and deals 6d6 Force damage. Complete destruction or transmutation of the stone expels you and deals 50 Force damage. If expelled, you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to your entry point and are Prone.

Name: Acid Arrow
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: A shimmering green arrow streaks toward a target within range and bursts in a spray of acid. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 4d4 Acid damage and 2d4 Acid damage at the end of its next turn. On a miss, the arrow splashes the target for half the initial damage only.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage (both initial and later) increases by 1d4 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Mending
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch—such as a broken chain link, a torn cloak, a cracked key, or a leaking wineskin. If the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, it is fully repaired with no trace of damage.

The spell can physically repair a magic item, but it cannot restore its magical properties.

Name: Message
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You point at a creature within range and whisper a message. Only the target hears the message and may whisper a reply that only you can hear.

You may cast this spell through solid objects if you know the target is beyond them. Magical silence, 1 foot of stone, metal, or wood, or a thin sheet of lead blocks the spell.

Name: Meteor Swarm
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
Four blazing meteors fall at points you choose within range. Each creature in a 40-foot-radius Sphere centered on each point must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 20d6 Fire damage and 20d6 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A creature in multiple Spheres is affected only once.

Nonmagical objects not worn or carried also take the damage and ignite if flammable.

Name: Mind Blank
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text: One willing creature you touch gains Immunity to Psychic damage and the Charmed condition for the duration. The target is also immune to effects that sense emotions or alignment, read thoughts, or magically detect its location. No spell—not even Wish—can gather information about the target, observe it remotely, or control its mind.

Name: Mind Sliver
Subclasses: Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You attempt to fracture the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw, taking 1d6 Psychic damage on a failed save and subtracting 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Mind Spike
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric
Text: You drive a psionic spike into the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, taking 3d8 Psychic damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. On a failed save, you also always know the target’s location for the duration while on the same plane. The target cannot hide from you, and if it is Invisible, it gains no benefit from that condition against you.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Minor Illusion
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Illusion (PHB'14) Wizard, Scion of the Three (FRHoF) Rogue, Shadow (PHB'24) Monk, Shadow (PHB'14) Monk
Text:
You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion ends if you cast this spell again.

A creature may take the Study action to examine the illusion. On a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC, it recognizes the illusion, which becomes faint to that creature.

Sound. You may create any sound from a whisper to a scream, including voices, animal noises, or other effects. The sound may be continuous or intermittent.

Image. You create an image of an object no larger than a 5-foot Cube. The image cannot produce sound, light, smell, or other sensory effects. Physical interaction reveals the illusion, as objects pass through it.

Name: Mirage Arcane
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You alter terrain in an area up to 1 mile square to look, sound, smell, and feel like another type of terrain. Open fields may appear as swamps, cliffs as gentle slopes, ponds as meadows, and so on.

You may also alter the appearance of structures or create illusory ones. The spell does not disguise or create creatures.

The illusion includes visual, auditory, tactile, and olfactory elements, allowing you to create Difficult Terrain or remove it. Any illusory object removed from the area vanishes instantly.

Creatures with Truesight see the true terrain beneath the illusion, but all other sensory elements remain, allowing physical interaction with the illusory features.

Name: Mirror Image
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Glamour (PHB'24) Bard, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Three illusory duplicates of yourself appear in your space. Until the spell ends, the duplicates move with you, mimic your actions, and constantly shift position, making it impossible to determine which image is real.

Each time a creature hits you with an attack roll, roll a d6 for each remaining duplicate. If any die shows 3 or higher, a duplicate is hit instead of you and is destroyed. Duplicates ignore all other damage and effects. The spell ends when all three duplicates are gone.

A creature is unaffected by this spell if it has the Blinded condition, Blindsight, or Truesight.

Name: Mislead
Subclasses: Fey Wanderer (TCE) Ranger, Fey Wanderer (PHB'24) Ranger, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You become Invisible at the same moment an illusory double appears where you were standing. The double lasts for the duration, but your invisibility ends immediately after you make an attack roll, deal damage, or cast a spell.

As a Magic action, you may move the double up to twice your Speed and make it gesture, speak, and behave however you choose. It is intangible and invulnerable.

You can see and hear through the double as though you were in its space.

Name: Misty Step
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Land (Temperate Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Fey Wanderer (PHB'24) Ranger, Fey Wanderer (TCE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin
Text: A burst of silvery mist surrounds you as you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

Name: Modify Memory
Subclasses: Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You attempt to reshape a creature’s memories. One creature you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw, with Advantage if you are fighting it. On a failed save, the target becomes Charmed for the duration, is Incapacitated, and is unaware of its surroundings, though it can still hear you. If it takes damage or is targeted by another spell, this spell ends with no memory alteration.

While the charm lasts, you may alter the target’s memory of an event it experienced within the last 24 hours that lasted no more than 10 minutes. You may erase the memory, clarify it, change its details, or implant a different memory.

You must verbally describe the altered memory, and the target must understand your language. If the spell ends before you finish describing the memory, no change occurs. Otherwise, the modified memory takes hold when the spell ends.

Illogical or contradictory memories may be dismissed by the target. Remove Curse or Greater Restoration restores the creature’s true memory.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can alter the target's memories of an event that took place up to 7 days ago (level 6 spell slot), 30 days ago (level 7 spell slot), 365 days ago (level 8 spell slot), or any time in the creature's past (level 9 spell slot).

Name: Moonbeam
Subclasses: Moon (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text:
A pale silvery beam shines down in a 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. Dim Light fills the Cylinder. On later turns, you may take a Magic action to move the Cylinder up to 60 feet.

When the Cylinder appears, each creature inside must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d10 Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. A shape-shifted creature (such as one affected by Polymorph) that fails the save reverts to its true form and cannot shape-shift again until it leaves the Cylinder.

A creature must also make this save when the Cylinder moves into its space, when it enters the area, or when it ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Faithful Hound
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter
Text:
You conjure a phantom watchdog in an unoccupied space you can see within range. It remains for the duration or until you and it are more than 300 feet apart.

Only you can see the hound. It is intangible and invulnerable. When a Small or larger creature comes within 30 feet without speaking the password you set when casting the spell, the hound begins barking loudly. It has Truesight out to 30 feet.

At the start of each of your turns, the hound attempts to bite one enemy within 5 feet. That creature must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d8 Force damage.

On later turns, you may take a Magic action to move the hound up to 30 feet.

Name: Magnificent Mansion
Components: V, S, M (a miniature door worth 15+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You conjure a shimmering door within range that lasts for the duration. The door leads to an extradimensional dwelling and measures 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. You and creatures you designate may enter while the door remains open. You may open or close it (no action required) while within 30 feet. When closed, the door is imperceptible.

Inside is a grand foyer with numerous chambers. The atmosphere is clean, warm, and fresh. You may design the floor plan, up to 50 contiguous 10-foot Cubes. The dwelling is furnished and decorated as you choose and contains enough food for a nine-course banquet for up to 100 people. Objects created by the spell vanish into smoke if removed.

A staff of 100 translucent servants attends the occupants. They obey your commands, cannot attack, and cannot leave the dwelling.

When the spell ends, all creatures and objects inside are expelled into the nearest unoccupied spaces outside the entrance.

Name: Private Sanctum
Components: V, S, M (a thin sheet of lead)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter
Text:
You ward an area within range, forming a Cube from 5 to 100 feet on each side. The spell lasts for the duration.

When casting, choose any of the following security properties:

• Sound cannot pass through the barrier.
• The barrier appears dark and foggy, blocking all vision through it.
• Divination spell sensors cannot appear inside or pass through the barrier.
• Creatures inside cannot be targeted by Divination spells.
• Nothing can teleport into or out of the area.
• Planar travel is blocked within the area.

Casting this spell on the same spot every day for 365 days makes the effect permanent until dispelled.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can increase the size of the Cube by 100 feet for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Arcane Sword
Components: V, S, M (a miniature sword worth 250+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create a spectral sword that hovers within range for the duration.

When it appears, you make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of the sword. On a hit, the target takes 4d12 + your spellcasting ability modifier Force damage.

On later turns, you may use a Bonus Action to move the sword up to 30 feet to a point you can see and repeat the attack.

Name: Move Earth
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
Choose an area of dirt, sand, or clay up to 40 feet on a side within range. You reshape the terrain in any manner you choose. You may raise or lower elevation, create or fill trenches, form or flatten walls, or create pillars. No change may exceed half the area’s largest dimension (e.g., a 40-foot square allows changes up to 20 feet high or deep). The transformation takes 10 minutes to complete. Because the change is gradual, creatures are not normally trapped or harmed.

Every 10 minutes you maintain Concentration, you may choose a new area within range to reshape.

This spell cannot affect natural stone or constructed stone. Structures shift to accommodate the new terrain and may collapse if destabilized.

Plants are not directly affected, but are carried along with the moved earth.

Name: Nondetection
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of diamond dust worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric
Text: For the duration, you hide a touched target from Divination magic. The target may be a willing creature, or a place or object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension. The target cannot be perceived by Divination spells or magical scrying sensors.

Name: Magic Aura
Components: V, S, M (a small square of silk)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
With a touch, you place an illusion on a willing creature or an unattended object. A creature gains the Mask effect, and an object gains the False Aura effect. The effect lasts for the duration. If you cast this spell on the same target every day for 30 days, the illusion becomes permanent until dispelled.

Mask (Creature). Choose a creature type other than the target’s actual type. Spells and magical effects treat the target as if it were the chosen type.

False Aura (Object). You alter how the object appears to magic that detects auras, such as Detect Magic. You may make a nonmagical object appear magical, make a magic item appear nonmagical, or change the item’s aura to any school of magic you choose.

Name: Freezing Sphere
Components: V, S, M (a miniature crystal sphere)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A frigid globe streaks to a point within range and explodes in a 60-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in the area must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d6 Cold damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

If the globe strikes a body of water, it freezes a 30-foot-square area to a depth of 6 inches for 1 minute. Creatures swimming on the surface become trapped and gain the Restrained condition. A trapped creature may use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC to break free.

You may choose not to fire the globe after casting. If so, a cold sling-bullet-sized globe appears in your hand. You or another creature may throw it (range 40 feet) or sling it (normal sling range). It shatters on impact with the spell’s normal effect. You may also set it down without breaking it. After 1 minute, if not shattered, it explodes automatically.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Resilient Sphere
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric
Text:
A shimmering sphere encloses a Large or smaller creature or object within range. An unwilling creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be enclosed.

Nothing—physical, magical, or otherwise—can pass through the barrier. A creature inside can breathe normally. The sphere is immune to all damage, and nothing inside can be harmed by effects originating outside, nor can anything inside harm anything outside.

The sphere is weightless and just large enough to contain its occupant. A creature inside may use an action to push against the interior and roll the sphere up to half its Speed. Other creatures may pick up and move the sphere.

A Disintegrate spell targeting the sphere destroys it without harming its contents.

Name: Irresistible Dance
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
One creature you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the creature dances comically until the end of its next turn, using all its movement to dance in place.

On a failed save, the creature becomes Charmed for the duration. While Charmed, it dances in place, must use all its movement to do so, has Disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws and attack rolls, and attack rolls against it have Advantage. On each of its turns, it may use an action to repeat the save, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Pass without Trace
Components: V, S, M (ashes from burned mistletoe)
Subclasses: Land (Grassland) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Trickery (PHB'24) Cleric, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric, Shadow (PHB'14) Monk
Text: You radiate a concealing aura in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. You and creatures you choose within the aura gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks and leave no tracks.

Name: Passwall
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of sesame seeds)
Subclasses: Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid
Text:
A passage opens on a wooden, plaster, or stone surface you can see within range. The opening may be up to 5 feet wide, 8 feet tall, and 20 feet deep. The passage does not weaken or destabilize the structure.

When the spell ends, any creatures or objects still inside the passage are safely ejected into the nearest unoccupied space adjacent to the surface.

Name: Phantasmal Force
Components: V, S, M (a bit of fleece)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock
Text:
You craft an illusion inside the mind of a creature you can see within range. The target makes an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, you create a phantasmal object, creature, or phenomenon no larger than a 10-foot Cube, perceivable only by the target. The phantasm includes sound, temperature, and other sensory details.

The target may take the Study action to examine the phantasm. On a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC, the spell ends.

While affected, the target treats the phantasm as real and rationalizes impossible outcomes. A phantasmal hazard or creature may deal 2d8 Psychic damage to the target on each of your turns if the target is within 5 feet of it or inside its area. The target perceives the damage as appropriate to the illusion.

Name: Phantasmal Killer
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text:
You tap into a creature’s nightmares and manifest an illusion of its deepest fears, visible only to it. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 4d10 Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls for the duration. On a successful save, it takes half damage and the spell ends.

At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save. On a failed save, it takes the Psychic damage again. On a success, the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d10 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Phantom Steed
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock
Text:
A Large, quasi-real, horse-like creature appears in an unoccupied space within range. You choose its appearance. It is equipped with a saddle, bit, and bridle. Any equipment created by the spell vanishes in smoke if carried more than 10 feet from the steed.

For the duration, you or a creature you choose may ride the steed. It uses the Riding Horse stat block but has a Speed of 100 feet and can travel 13 miles per hour. When the spell ends, the steed fades over 1 minute, giving the rider time to dismount. The spell ends early if the steed takes any damage.

Name: Planar Ally
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You call upon a known otherworldly entity—such as a god, demon prince, or other cosmic being—for aid. That entity sends a loyal Celestial, Elemental, or Fiend, which appears in an unoccupied space within range. If you know a specific creature’s name, you may request it, though the DM may send a different creature.

The creature is not compelled to obey you. You may negotiate for its service, offering payment. Tasks may be simple (carry us across a chasm, aid us in battle) or complex (spy on enemies, protect us during an expedition). You must be able to communicate with the creature.

Payment varies by creature. A Celestial may require valuable offerings to a temple; a Fiend may demand treasure or a living sacrifice. Some may request a quest.

Tasks measured in minutes cost 100 GP per minute; tasks measured in hours cost 1,000 GP per hour; tasks measured in days (up to 10 days) cost 10,000 GP per day. The DM may adjust costs based on circumstances or alignment. Nonhazardous tasks often cost half; dangerous tasks may cost more. Creatures rarely accept suicidal tasks.

When the task is complete or the agreed duration ends, the creature returns to its home plane after reporting back if possible. If no agreement is reached, it immediately departs.

Name: Planar Binding
Components: V, S, M (a jewel worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You attempt to bind a Celestial, Elemental, Fey, or Fiend to your service. The creature must remain within range for the entire casting. (Typically, it is first trapped inside an inverted Magic Circle.) At the end of the casting, the target must make a Charisma saving throw or be bound to serve you for the duration. If the creature was summoned by another spell, that spell’s duration becomes equal to this spell’s duration.

A bound creature must follow your commands to the best of its ability. You may command it to accompany you, guard a location, deliver a message, and so on. A Hostile creature may twist your commands to suit its own goals.

If it completes your commands before the spell ends, it travels to you to report if you are on the same plane. If you are on another plane, it returns to the place where it was bound and waits until the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The duration increases with a spell slot of level 6 (10 days), 7 (30 days), 8 (180 days), and 9 (366 days).

Name: Plane Shift
Components: V, S, M (a forked, metal rod worth 250+ GP and attuned to a plane of existence)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to eight willing creatures who link hands are transported to another plane of existence. You may specify a general destination—such as the City of Brass or the palace of Dispater—and you appear in or near that location, as determined by the DM.

Alternatively, if you know the sigil sequence of a teleportation circle on another plane, the spell transports you to that circle. If the circle is too small to contain all transported creatures, they appear in the nearest unoccupied spaces.

Name: Plant Growth
Subclasses: Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
This spell channels vitality into plants. The casting time determines whether you create Overgrowth or Enrichment.

Overgrowth. Choose a point within range. All normal plants in a 100-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point grow thick and overgrown. Moving through the area costs 4 feet of movement per 1 foot traveled. You may exclude any number of areas within the Sphere.

Enrichment. All plants in a half-mile radius centered on a point within range become enriched for 365 days, producing twice the normal amount of food. Plants can benefit from only one Plant Growth per year.

Name: Poison Spray
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You spray toxic mist at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d12 Poison damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d12 when you reach levels 5 (2d12), 11 (3d12), and 17 (4d12).

Name: Polymorph
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Trickery (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You attempt to transform a creature you can see within range into a Beast. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or be shape-shifted for the duration. The Beast form may be any Beast with a Challenge Rating equal to or lower than the target’s (or its level if it has no CR). The target’s statistics are replaced by the Beast’s stat block, but it retains its alignment, personality, creature type, Hit Points, and Hit Point Dice.

The target gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the Beast’s Hit Points. These vanish when the spell ends. The spell ends early if the target has no Temporary Hit Points remaining.

The target is limited by the anatomy of its new form and cannot speak or cast spells.

Its equipment melds into the new form and cannot be used.

Name: Power Word Fortify
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: You fortify up to six creatures you can see within range. You grant a total of 120 Temporary Hit Points, divided among the chosen creatures as you wish.

Name: Power Word Heal
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: A wave of healing energy washes over a creature you can see within range. The target regains all its Hit Points. If it is Charmed, Frightened, Paralyzed, Poisoned, or Stunned, that condition ends. If it is Prone, it may use its Reaction to stand.

Name: Power Word Kill
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text: You compel a creature you can see within range to die. If it has 100 Hit Points or fewer, it dies instantly. Otherwise, it takes 12d12 Psychic damage.

Name: Power Word Stun
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You overwhelm the mind of a creature you can see within range. If it has 150 Hit Points or fewer, it becomes Stunned. Otherwise, its Speed becomes 0 until the start of your next turn.

A Stunned creature repeats a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success.

Name: Prayer of Healing
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: Up to five creatures of your choice that remain within range for the entire casting gain the benefits of a Short Rest and also regain 2d8 Hit Points. A creature cannot benefit from this spell again until it finishes a Long Rest.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The healing increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Prestidigitation
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Archer (XGE) Fighter, Arcane Archer (XGE) Fighter, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
You create a minor magical effect within range. Choose one option below. If you cast this spell multiple times, you may have up to three non-instantaneous effects active at once.

Sensory Effect. You create an instantaneous, harmless sensory effect such as sparks, a puff of wind, faint music, or an odd smell.

Fire Play. You instantly light or extinguish a candle, torch, or small campfire.

Clean or Soil. You instantly clean or soil an object no larger than 1 cubic foot.

Minor Sensation. You chill, warm, or flavor up to 1 cubic foot of nonliving material for 1 hour.

Magic Mark. You place a color, mark, or symbol on an object or surface for 1 hour.

Minor Creation. You create a nonmagical trinket or an illusory handheld image. It lasts until the end of your next turn and has no value or damaging capability.

Name: Prismatic Spray
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
Eight rays of light burst from you in a 60-foot Cone. Each creature in the Cone makes a Dexterity saving throw. For each target, roll 1d8 to determine which color ray affects it, using the Prismatic Rays table.

Prismatic Rays
1d8 — Ray
1 Red. Failed Save: 12d6 Fire damage. Successful Save: Half damage.
2 Orange. Failed Save: 12d6 Acid damage. Successful Save: Half damage.
3 Yellow. Failed Save: 12d6 Lightning damage. Successful Save: Half damage.
4 Green. Failed Save: 12d6 Poison damage. Successful Save: Half damage.
5 Blue. Failed Save: 12d6 Cold damage. Successful Save: Half damage.
6 Indigo. Failed Save: Restrained. At the end of each turn, the target makes a Constitution save. Three successes end the effect; three failures cause Petrified until freed by Greater Restoration or similar magic.
7 Violet. Failed Save: Blinded. At the start of your next turn, the target makes a Wisdom save. On a success, the effect ends. On a failure, the effect ends and the creature is teleported to another plane (DM’s choice).
8 Special. The target is struck by two rays. Roll twice, rerolling any 8.

Name: Prismatic Wall
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
A shimmering, multicolored wall of light forms a vertical opaque barrier up to 90 feet long, 30 feet high, and 1 inch thick, centered on a point within range. Alternatively, you may form a globe up to 30 feet in diameter. If the wall overlaps a creature’s space, the spell ends with no effect.

The wall sheds Bright Light for 100 feet and Dim Light for another 100 feet. You and creatures you designate may pass through safely. Any other creature that can see the wall and moves within 20 feet of it or starts its turn there must make a Constitution saving throw or be Blinded for 1 minute.

The wall has seven layers, each with unique effects. A creature passing through encounters each layer in order, making a Dexterity saving throw for each. Each layer can be destroyed only by specific means.

Prismatic Layers
1 Red. Failed Save: 12d6 Fire damage; half on success. Nonmagical ranged attacks cannot pass. Destroyed by 25 Cold damage.
2 Orange. Failed Save: 12d6 Acid damage; half on success. Magical ranged attacks cannot pass. Destroyed by strong wind (e.g., Gust of Wind).
3 Yellow. Failed Save: 12d6 Lightning damage; half on success. Destroyed by 60 Force damage.
4 Green. Failed Save: 12d6 Poison damage; half on success. Destroyed by Passwall or similar magic.
5 Blue. Failed Save: 12d6 Cold damage; half on success. Destroyed by 25 Fire damage.
6 Indigo. Failed Save: Restrained; then Con saves each turn—three successes end it, three failures cause Petrified. Spells cannot pass. Destroyed by Bright Light from Daylight.
7 Violet. Failed Save: Blinded; then Wis save next turn—success ends it, failure teleports the creature to another plane. Destroyed by Dispel Magic.

Name: Produce Flame
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A flickering flame appears in your hand for the duration. It emits no heat, ignites nothing, and sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for another 20 feet. The spell ends if you cast it again.

Until then, you may take a Magic action to hurl the flame at a creature or object within 60 feet. Make a ranged spell attack; on a hit, the target takes 1d8 Fire damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Programmed Illusion
Components: V, S, M (jade dust worth 25+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create an illusion of an object, creature, or visible phenomenon within range that activates when a trigger occurs. The illusion is imperceptible until triggered. It must fit within a 30-foot Cube, and you script its behavior and sounds for up to 5 minutes.

When the trigger occurs, the illusion appears and performs as scripted. After finishing, it disappears and remains dormant for 10 minutes before it can trigger again.

The trigger must be based on visual or audible events within 30 feet of the area.

Physical interaction reveals the illusion. A creature may Study it with an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to see through it; if successful, the illusion becomes faint and its sounds hollow.

Name: Project Image
Components: V, S, M (a statuette of yourself worth 5+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create an illusory duplicate of yourself that lasts for the duration. It may appear at any location within range that you have previously seen, regardless of obstacles. The illusion looks and sounds like you but is intangible. If it takes any damage, it vanishes and the spell ends.

You can see and hear through the illusion as if you were in its space. As a Magic action, you may move it up to 60 feet and make it gesture, speak, and behave as you choose.

Physical interaction reveals the illusion. A creature may Study it with an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC to see through it; if successful, the illusion becomes faint and its sounds hollow.

Name: Protection from Energy
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Cleric (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Ranger (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Glory (PHB'24) Paladin, Glory (TCE) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: For the duration, a willing creature you touch gains Resistance to one damage type of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder.

Name: Protection from Evil and Good
Components: V, S, M (a flask of Holy Water worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin
Text: Until the spell ends, a willing creature you touch is protected from Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead. These creatures have Disadvantage on attack rolls against the target. The target cannot be possessed or gain the Charmed or Frightened conditions from them. If already affected, the target has Advantage on new saving throws against those effects.

Name: Protection from Poison
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You touch a creature and end the Poisoned condition on it. For the duration, the target has Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Poisoned condition and has Resistance to Poison damage.

Name: Purify Food and Drink
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: You purify nonmagical food and drink in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range, removing poison and rot.

Name: Raise Dead
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 500+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You touch a dead creature that has been dead no more than 10 days and was not Undead. The creature returns to life with 1 Hit Point. Any poisons affecting it at the time of death are neutralized.

The spell closes mortal wounds but does not restore missing body parts. If the creature lacks essential organs or limbs—such as its head—the spell automatically fails.

Returning from death is taxing. The creature has a –4 penalty to all D20 Tests. Each time it finishes a Long Rest, the penalty decreases by 1 until it reaches 0.

Name: Telepathic Bond
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You forge a telepathic bond among up to eight willing creatures within range. Creatures that cannot communicate in any language are unaffected.

For the duration, the linked creatures can communicate telepathically with one another regardless of distance, though not across different planes.

Name: Ray of Enfeeblement
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A beam of weakening energy shoots toward a creature within range. The target makes a Constitution saving throw.

On a successful save, it has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

On a failed save, it has Disadvantage on Strength-based D20 Tests for the duration and subtracts 1d8 from all its damage rolls. At the end of each of its turns, it repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Ray of Frost
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: A frigid beam of blue-white light streaks toward a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Cold damage and its Speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Ray of Sickness
Subclasses: Alchemist (TCE) Artificer, Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text: You fire a sickly green ray at a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 Poison damage and becomes Poisoned until the end of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Regenerate
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: You touch a creature, restoring 4d8 + 15 Hit Points. For the duration, the target regains 1 Hit Point at the start of each of its turns, and any severed body parts regrow after 2 minutes.

Name: Reincarnate
Components: V, S, M (rare oils worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Text:
You touch a dead Humanoid or a piece of one. If it has been dead no more than 10 days, the spell creates a new body and calls the soul to inhabit it. Roll 1d10 to determine the new species, or the DM chooses another playable species.

1 Aasimar
2 Dragonborn
3 Dwarf
4 Elf
5 Gnome
6 Goliath
7 Halfling
8 Human
9 Orc
10 Tiefling

The reincarnated creature makes any species-specific choices and remembers its past life. It retains its previous capabilities except it loses its old species traits and gains those of its new one.

Name: Remove Curse
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Winter Walker (FRHoF) Ranger
Text: You touch a creature or object and end all curses affecting it. If the object is a cursed magic item, the curse remains, but the spell breaks the owner’s Attunement so the item can be removed.

Name: Resistance
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You touch a willing creature and choose a damage type: Acid, Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Piercing, Poison, Radiant, Slashing, or Thunder. The first time the creature takes damage of that type before the spell ends, it reduces the damage by 1d4. A creature can benefit from this spell only once per turn.

Name: Resurrection
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You touch a dead creature that has been dead no more than 100 years, did not die of old age, and was not Undead. The creature returns to life with all its Hit Points. Any poisons affecting it at the time of death are neutralized. The spell closes all mortal wounds and restores any missing body parts.

Returning from death is taxing. The creature has a –4 penalty to all D20 Tests. Each time it finishes a Long Rest, the penalty decreases by 1 until it reaches 0.

If you use this spell to revive a creature that has been dead for 365 days or more, you are severely strained: until you finish a Long Rest, you cannot cast spells and you have Disadvantage on all D20 Tests.

Name: Reverse Gravity
Components: V, S, M (a lodestone and iron filings)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
This spell reverses gravity in a 50-foot-radius, 100-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point within range. All creatures and objects in the area that are not anchored fall upward to the top of the Cylinder. A creature may make a Dexterity saving throw to grab a fixed object and avoid rising.

If a ceiling or anchored object is encountered, creatures and objects strike it as they would during a normal fall. If nothing is struck, they hover at the top for the duration. When the spell ends, they fall downward.

Name: Revivify
Components: V, S, M (a diamond worth 300+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Life (PHB'24) Cleric, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text: You touch a creature that has died within the last minute. It returns to life with 1 Hit Point. This spell cannot revive a creature that died of old age and does not restore missing body parts.

Name: Rope Trick
Components: V, S, M (a segment of rope)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Gloom Stalker (PHB'24) Ranger, Gloom Stalker (XGE) Ranger
Text:
You touch a rope. One end rises until the rope hangs vertically or reaches a ceiling. At the upper end, an Invisible 3-by-5-foot portal opens into an extradimensional space that lasts for the duration. The space is accessible by climbing the rope, which may be pulled inside or dropped out.

The space can hold up to eight Medium or smaller creatures. No attacks, spells, or effects can pass into or out of it, though creatures inside can see through the portal. When the spell ends, everything inside falls out into the nearest unoccupied space.

Name: Sacred Flame
Subclasses: Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock
Text: A beam of radiant flame descends on a creature you can see within range. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d8 Radiant damage. The target gains no benefit from Half Cover or Three-Quarters Cover for this save.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Sanctuary
Components: V, S, M (a shard of glass from a mirror)
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Open Hand (PHB'14) Monk
Text:
You ward a creature within range. Until the spell ends, any creature that targets the warded creature with an attack roll or damaging spell must make a Wisdom saving throw or choose a new target or lose the attack or spell. This spell does not protect against areas of effect.

The spell ends if the warded creature makes an attack roll, casts a spell, or deals damage.

Name: Scorching Ray
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Wildfire (TCE) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Efreeti) (TCE) Warlock
Text: You hurl three rays of fire. You may direct them at one or multiple targets within range. Make a ranged spell attack for each ray. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 Fire damage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You create one additional ray for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Scrying
Components: V, S, M (a focus worth 1,000+ GP, such as a crystal ball, mirror, or water-filled font)
Subclasses: Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Vengeance (PHB'14) Paladin, Vengeance (PHB'24) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin
Text:
You perceive a creature on the same plane of existence. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw, modified by how well you know it and any physical connection you possess.

Knowledge of Target:
Secondhand (heard of): +5
Firsthand (met): +0
Extensive (know well): –5

Possession of Target’s Item:
Picture or likeness: –2
Garment or possession: –4
Body part, hair, or nail: –10

On a successful save, the target is unaffected and you cannot target it again for 24 hours.

On a failed save, an Invisible sensor appears within 10 feet of the target. You can see and hear through it as if present. The sensor moves with the target. If visible, it appears as a fist-sized luminous orb.

Instead of a creature, you may target a location you have seen; the sensor appears there and remains stationary.

Name: Searing Smite
Subclasses: Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: When you hit a creature with an attack, it takes an extra 1d6 Fire damage. At the start of each of its turns, it takes 1d6 Fire damage and then makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the effect continues; on a success, the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. All the damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: See Invisibility
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of talc)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Watchers (TCE) Paladin, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text: For the duration, you see creatures and objects with the Invisible condition as though visible, and you can see into the Ethereal Plane. Creatures and objects there appear ghostly.

Name: Seeming
Subclasses: Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Gloom Stalker (XGE) Ranger, Gloom Stalker (PHB'24) Ranger, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You alter the appearance of any number of creatures you can see within range. An unwilling creature may make a Charisma saving throw; on a success, it is unaffected.

You may give all targets the same appearance or different ones. You can change the appearance of their bodies and equipment, making them up to 1 foot taller or shorter and altering apparent weight. The new appearance must keep the same basic limb arrangement, but otherwise the illusion is up to you.

The illusion does not withstand physical inspection—objects pass through illusory additions such as clothing or accessories.

A creature may take the Study action to examine a disguised target. On a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC, it realizes the target is disguised.

Name: Sending
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You send a message of 25 words or fewer to a creature you have met or that has been described to you by someone who has met it. The target hears the message in its mind, recognizes you if it knows you, and may reply immediately in the same manner. The spell allows the target to understand your meaning.

You may send the message across any distance and even to other planes. If the target is on another plane, there is a 5% chance the message fails to arrive; you know if it fails.

After receiving your message, the target may block you from contacting it with this spell for 8 hours. If you attempt to send another message during that time, you learn you are blocked and the spell fails.

Name: Sequester
Components: V, S, M (gem dust worth 5,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You touch an object or willing creature and magically sequester it. For the duration, the target becomes Invisible, cannot be targeted by Divination spells, cannot be detected by magic, and cannot be viewed remotely.

If the target is a creature, it enters suspended animation: it becomes Unconscious, does not age, and requires no food, water, or air.

You may set a condition for the spell to end early. The condition must occur or be observable within 1 mile of the target. Examples include “after 1,000 years” or “when the tarrasque awakens.” The spell also ends if the target takes any damage.

Name: Shapechange
Components: V, S, M (a jade circlet worth 1,500+ GP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You shape-shift into another creature for the duration, or until you take a Magic action to change into another eligible form. The form must be a creature you have seen, with a Challenge Rating no higher than your level or CR, and it cannot be a Construct or Undead.

When you first shape-shift, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the Hit Points of the chosen form. These vanish when the spell ends. The spell ends early if you have no Temporary Hit Points remaining.

Your game statistics are replaced by the form’s stat block, but you retain your creature type, alignment, personality, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, Hit Points, Hit Dice, proficiencies, communication ability, and Spellcasting feature (if you have it).

When you transform, you choose whether your equipment drops to the ground or reshapes to fit your new form.

Name: Shatter
Components: V, S, M (a chip of mica)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A loud detonation erupts from a point you choose within range. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d8 Thunder damage on a failed save or half as much on a success. Constructs have Disadvantage on the save.

Nonmagical objects not worn or carried also take the damage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Shield
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: A barrier of magical force surrounds you. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +5 bonus to AC (including against the triggering attack), and you take no damage from Magic Missile.

Name: Shield of Faith
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, War (PHB'24) Cleric, War (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: A shimmering field surrounds a creature you choose within range, granting it a +2 bonus to AC for the duration.

Name: Shillelagh
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
A Club or Quarterstaff you hold is infused with primal power. For the duration, you may use your spellcasting ability instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls with the weapon, and its damage die becomes a d8. On a hit, the damage may be Force damage or the weapon’s normal type (your choice).

The spell ends early if you cast it again or release the weapon.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage die changes when you reach levels 5 (d10), 11 (d12), and 17 (2d6).

Name: Shining Smite
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: The target you hit takes an extra 2d6 Radiant damage. Until the spell ends, the target sheds Bright Light in a 5-foot radius, attack rolls against it have Advantage, and it cannot benefit from the Invisible condition.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Shocking Grasp
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Temperate Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: Lightning leaps from your hand toward a creature you attempt to touch. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Lightning damage and cannot make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Silence
Subclasses: Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Shadow (PHB'14) Monk
Text: For the duration, no sound can be created within or pass through a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range. Any creature or object entirely inside the Sphere has Immunity to Thunder damage, and creatures inside are Deafened. Spells with Verbal components cannot be cast in the Sphere.

Name: Silent Image
Components: V, S, M (a bit of fleece)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create the image of an object, creature, or visible phenomenon no larger than a 15-foot Cube. The image appears at a point within range and lasts for the duration. It is purely visual—no sound, smell, or other sensory effects.

As a Magic action, you may move the image to another point within range and alter its appearance to make the movement look natural.

Physical interaction reveals the illusion, as objects pass through it. A creature may take the Study action to examine the image; on a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC, it recognizes the illusion and can see through it.

Name: Simulacrum
Components: V, S, M (powdered ruby worth 1,500+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create a simulacrum of a Beast or Humanoid that remains within 10 feet of you for the entire casting. You complete the spell by touching the creature and a same-sized pile of ice or snow, which transforms into the simulacrum.

The simulacrum uses the original creature’s statistics at the time of casting, except it is a Construct, has half the original’s Hit Point maximum, and cannot cast this spell.

It is Friendly to you and designated creatures, obeys your commands, and acts on your turn. It cannot gain levels or take Short or Long Rests.

Damage can be repaired only during your Long Rest by expending components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored, and the simulacrum must remain within 5 feet of you.

It lasts until reduced to 0 Hit Points, at which point it becomes snow and melts. Casting this spell again destroys any existing simulacrum you created.

Name: Sleep
Components: V, S, M (a pinch of sand or rose petals)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Archfey (PHB'24) Warlock, Land (Temperate Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Archfey (PHB'14) Warlock, Twilight (TCE) Cleric, Twilight (TCE) Cleric
Text:
