Each creature of your choice in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes Incapacitated until the end of its next turn, then repeats the save. On a second failed save, it becomes Unconscious for the duration.

The spell ends for a creature if it takes damage or if someone within 5 feet uses an action to shake it awake.

Creatures that do not sleep—such as elves—or that have Immunity to Exhaustion automatically succeed on the saves.

Name: Sleet Storm
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Polar Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Marid) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Sleet pours down in a 40-foot-tall, 20-foot-radius Cylinder centered on a point within range. The area is Heavily Obscured, and exposed flames are extinguished.

The ground becomes Difficult Terrain. When a creature enters the Cylinder for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Dexterity saving throw or fall Prone and lose Concentration.

Name: Slow
Components: V, S, M (a drop of molasses)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You warp time around up to six creatures of your choice in a 40-foot Cube within range. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw or be affected for the duration.

An affected creature’s Speed is halved, it takes a –2 penalty to AC and Dexterity saving throws, and it cannot take Reactions. On its turn, it may take either an action or a Bonus Action, not both, and it can make only one attack if it takes the Attack action.

If it casts a spell with a Somatic component, there is a 25% chance the spell fails due to slowed gestures.

At the end of each of its turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Sorcerous Burst
Text:
You cast sorcerous energy at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 damage of a type you choose: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, Psychic, or Thunder.

If you roll an 8 on the d8, you may roll another d8 and add it to the damage. The maximum number of extra d8s you may add equals your spellcasting ability modifier.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Spare the Dying
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Choose a creature within range that has 0 Hit Points and is not dead. The creature becomes Stable.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The range doubles when you reach levels 5 (30 feet), 11 (60 feet), and 17 (120 feet).

Name: Speak with Animals
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, Totem Warrior (PHB'14) Barbarian, Wild Heart (PHB'24) Barbarian
Text:
For the duration, you can understand and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you may use any Influence action skill options with them.

Most Beasts speak only about survival or companionship, but they can provide information about nearby locations, creatures, and events they have perceived within the last day.

Name: Speak with Dead
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undead (VRGR) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock, Undying (SCAG) Warlock
Text:
You grant a semblance of life to a corpse within range, allowing it to answer questions. The corpse must have a mouth, must not have been Undead when it died, and must not have been targeted by this spell in the last 10 days.

You may ask up to five questions. The corpse knows only what it knew in life, including languages. Answers are brief, cryptic, or repetitive, and the corpse is not compelled to be truthful if it dislikes you.

This spell does not return the soul—only animates the body’s spirit. The corpse cannot learn new information, does not understand events after its death, and cannot speculate about the future.

Name: Speak with Plants
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard
Text:
You grant limited sentience and animation to plants within a stationary 30-foot Emanation. The plants can communicate simple ideas to you and follow basic commands. You may question them about events in the area within the past day, learning about passing creatures, weather, and other notable occurrences.

You may also turn plant-based Difficult Terrain (such as thickets or undergrowth) into normal terrain for the duration, or turn normal plant-filled terrain into Difficult Terrain.

Plants cannot uproot themselves, but they can move branches, tendrils, and stalks. Plant creatures within the area can communicate with you as if you shared a language.

Name: Spider Climb
Components: V, S, M (a drop of bitumen and a spider)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: Until the spell ends, a willing creature you touch can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and ceilings without using its hands. The creature also gains a Climb Speed equal to its Speed.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2.

Name: Spike Growth
Subclasses: Land (Arctic) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
The ground in a 20-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range sprouts spikes and thorns. The area becomes Difficult Terrain for the duration. A creature that moves into or within the area takes 2d4 Piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels.

The altered ground is camouflaged to appear natural. A creature that cannot see the area when the spell is cast must take the Search action and succeed on a Wisdom (Perception or Survival) check against your spell save DC to recognize the hazard before entering.

Name: Spirit Guardians
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, War (PHB'24) Cleric, War (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
Protective spirits swirl around you in a 15-foot Emanation for the duration. If you are good or neutral, they appear angelic or fey (your choice); if evil, they appear fiendish.

You may designate creatures unaffected by the spell. Other creatures have their Speed halved in the Emanation. When the Emanation enters a creature’s space, or when a creature enters or ends its turn in it, the creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d8 Radiant damage (if you are good or neutral) or 3d8 Necrotic damage (if you are evil). On a successful save, it takes half damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Spiritual Weapon
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Life (PHB'14) Cleric, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, War (PHB'24) Cleric, War (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You create a floating spectral force shaped like a weapon of your choice. It appears within range, and you immediately make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier Force damage.

As a Bonus Action on later turns, you may move the weapon up to 20 feet and repeat the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for every slot level above 2.

Name: Staggering Smite
Subclasses: Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: The target hit by your attack takes an extra 4d6 Psychic damage and must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is Stunned until the end of your next turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The extra damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Starry Wisp
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Moon (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You launch a mote of radiant light at a creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Radiant damage and emits Dim Light in a 10-foot radius until the end of your next turn. During that time, it cannot benefit from the Invisible condition.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d8 when you reach levels 5 (2d8), 11 (3d8), and 17 (4d8).

Name: Steel Wind Strike
Components: S, M (a Melee weapon worth 1+ SP)
Subclasses: War (PHB'24) Cleric
Text:
You flourish the weapon used in the casting and vanish, striking with supernatural speed. Choose up to five creatures you can see within range. Make a melee spell attack against each. On a hit, a target takes 6d10 Force damage.

You then teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of one of the targets.

Name: Stinking Cloud
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of yellow, nauseating gas centered on a point within range. The area is Heavily Obscured. The cloud persists for the duration or until dispersed by a strong wind (such as Gust of Wind).

A creature that starts its turn in the cloud must make a Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned until the end of the current turn. While Poisoned in this way, the creature cannot take actions or Bonus Actions.

Name: Stone Shape
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock
Text: You touch a Medium or smaller stone object, or a section of stone no more than 5 feet in any dimension, and reshape it as you wish. You may form a rock into a weapon, statue, or container, or create a small passage through a stone wall up to 5 feet thick. You may also reshape a stone door or frame to seal it shut. The created object may include up to two hinges and a latch, but no finer mechanical detail is possible.

Name: Stoneskin
Components: V, S, M (diamond dust worth 100+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Conquest (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin, War (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Until the spell ends, a willing creature you touch gains Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

Name: Storm of Vengeance
Text:
A massive storm cloud forms for the duration, centered on a point within range and spreading to a 300-foot radius. When it appears, each creature beneath it must make a Constitution saving throw or take 2d6 Thunder damage and become Deafened for the duration.

At the start of each of your turns, the storm produces the following effects:

Turn 2. Acidic rain falls. Each creature and object under the cloud takes 4d6 Acid damage.

Turn 3. Six lightning bolts strike six different creatures or objects beneath the cloud. Each target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

Turn 4. Hailstones fall. Each creature under the cloud takes 2d6 Bludgeoning damage.

Turns 5–10. Gusts and freezing rain batter the area. Each creature takes 1d6 Cold damage. The area becomes Difficult Terrain and Heavily Obscured, ranged weapon attacks are impossible, and strong winds blow throughout.

Name: Suggestion
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Knowledge (PHB'14) Cleric
Text:
You suggest a course of action—described in 25 words or fewer—to a creature you can see within range that can hear and understand you. The suggestion must seem reasonable and cannot involve obvious harm to the target or its allies.

The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Charmed for the duration or until you or your allies damage it. A Charmed target follows the suggestion to the best of its ability. If the task can be completed sooner than the spell’s duration, the spell ends for that target once the task is done.

Name: Summon Aberration
Components: V, S, M (a pickled tentacle and an eyeball in a platinum-inlaid vial worth 400+ GP)
Subclasses: Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock
Text:
You summon an aberrant spirit in an unoccupied space within range. It uses the Aberrant Spirit stat block. When casting, choose Beholderkin, Mind Flayer, or Slaad; the spirit resembles that type, determining certain stat block details. It disappears when reduced to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.

The spirit is an ally to you and your allies. It shares your Initiative count but acts immediately after you. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required). If you give no commands, it takes the Dodge action and moves to avoid danger.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter

Name: Sunbeam
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You fire a beam of sunlight in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long Line. Each creature in the Line makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 6d8 Radiant damage and is Blinded until the start of your next turn; on a successful save, it takes half damage only.

Until the spell ends, you may take a Magic action to create a new Line of radiance.

A mote of brilliant sunlight hovers above you for the duration, shedding Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for another 30 feet. This light counts as sunlight.

Name: Sunburst
Components: V, S, M (a piece of sunstone)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A burst of brilliant sunlight erupts in a 60-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range. Each creature in the area makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 12d6 Radiant damage and is Blinded for 1 minute; on a successful save, it takes half damage only.

A creature Blinded by this spell repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

This spell dispels any magical Darkness in its area.

Name: Swift Quiver
Components: V, S, M (a Quiver worth 1+ GP)
Text: When you cast this spell and as a Bonus Action on later turns, you may make two attacks with a weapon that fires Arrows or Bolts, such as a Longbow or Light Crossbow. The spell creates the ammunition for each attack. Each piece of ammunition deals damage like a nonmagical version of its type and disintegrates after hitting or missing.

Name: Symbol
Components: V, S, M (powdered diamond worth 1,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Abjurer (PHB'24) Wizard, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You inscribe a harmful glyph on a surface or inside a closable object. The glyph can cover up to a 10-foot diameter. If inscribed inside an object, it must remain in place; moving it more than 10 feet breaks the glyph and ends the spell.

The glyph is nearly invisible and requires a successful Wisdom (Perception) check against your spell save DC to notice.

When inscribing the glyph, you set its trigger and choose one effect: Death, Discord, Fear, Pain, Sleep, or Stunning.

You determine the trigger conditions. You may refine the trigger to affect only certain creature types or exempt creatures meeting specific conditions.

When triggered, the glyph glows and fills a 60-foot-radius Sphere with Dim Light for 10 minutes. Each creature in the Sphere when it activates, or that enters or ends its turn there, is targeted by the effect (once per turn).

Death. Constitution save; 10d10 Necrotic damage on a failed save, half on a success.

Discord. Wisdom save; on a failure, the target argues incoherently for 1 minute, cannot communicate meaningfully, and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.

Fear. Wisdom save; on a failure, the target is Frightened for 1 minute and must move at least 30 feet away from the glyph each turn if able.

Pain. Constitution save; on a failure, the target is Incapacitated for 1 minute.

Sleep. Wisdom save; on a failure, the target is Unconscious for 10 minutes. It awakens if it takes damage or is shaken awake.

Stunning. Wisdom save; on a failure, the target is Stunned for 1 minute.

Name: Synaptic Static
Subclasses: Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric
Text:
You cause psychic energy to erupt at a point within range. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius Sphere makes an Intelligence saving throw, taking 8d6 Psychic damage on a failed save or half on a success.

On a failed save, the creature also has muddled thoughts for 1 minute, subtracting 1d6 from attack rolls, ability checks, and Constitution saves to maintain Concentration. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

Name: Bubbling Cauldron
Components: V, S, M (a gilded ladle worth 500 + GP)
Subclasses: Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You conjure a claw-footed cauldron filled with bubbling liquid. It appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you and lasts for the duration. The cauldron cannot be moved and disappears when the spell ends.

The liquid duplicates the effects of a Common or Uncommon potion of your choice. As a Bonus Action, you or an ally may withdraw one such potion from the cauldron. The potion appears in a vial that disappears after use.

The cauldron can produce a number of potions equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (minimum 1). When the last potion is taken, the cauldron vanishes and the spell ends.

Any unused potions disappear when you cast this spell again.

Name: Hideous Laughter
Components: V, S, M (a tart and a feather)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
One creature you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it becomes Prone and Incapacitated for the duration, laughing uncontrollably if capable. It cannot stand up while affected.

At the end of each of its turns, and whenever it takes damage, it repeats the save (with Advantage if triggered by damage). On a success, the spell ends.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Telekinesis
Subclasses: Aberrant Mind (TCE) Sorcerer, Aberrant (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Great Old One (PHB'24) Warlock, Psi Warrior (TCE) Fighter, Psi Warrior (PHB'24) Fighter, Great Old One (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You gain the ability to move or manipulate creatures or objects with your mind. When you cast the spell and as a Magic action on later turns, you may affect one creature or object within range.

Creature. You may attempt to move a Huge or smaller creature. It must make a Strength saving throw or be moved up to 30 feet in any direction. Until the end of your next turn, it is Restrained. If lifted, it remains suspended until the end of your next turn unless you target it again and it fails the save.

Object. You may move a Huge or smaller object up to 30 feet in any direction. If the object is worn or carried, its bearer must make a Strength saving throw or you pull it free and move it.

You may perform fine manipulation with your telekinetic grip, such as using a simple tool, opening a door or container, retrieving or stowing an item, or pouring a vial.

Name: Telepathy
Components: V, S, M (a pair of linked silver rings)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You establish a telepathic link between yourself and a willing creature you know. The creature may be anywhere on the same plane of existence. The spell ends if either of you leaves the plane.

For the duration, you and the target can instantly share words, images, sounds, and other sensory impressions. The target recognizes you as the sender. The spell allows the target to understand the meaning of your communication.

Name: Teleport
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
This spell instantly transports you and up to eight willing creatures you can see within range, or a single object you can see within range, to a destination you choose. If targeting an object, it must be Large or smaller and not held or carried by an unwilling creature.

The destination must be known to you and on the same plane. Your familiarity determines the accuracy of arrival. The DM rolls 1d100 and consults the Teleportation Outcome table.

Familiarity definitions:
Permanent circle — a permanent teleportation circle whose sigil sequence you know.
Linked object — an item taken from the destination within the last six months.
Very familiar — a place you know well, have studied, or can currently see.
Seen casually — a place you’ve seen more than once but don’t know well.
Viewed once or described — a place you’ve seen once or only know by description.
False destination — a place that does not exist or no longer exists.

Outcome explanations:
Mishap — each creature or object takes 3d10 Force damage; the DM rerolls on the table (multiple mishaps possible).
Similar Area — you arrive in a visually or thematically similar location.
Off Target — you arrive 2d12 miles away in a random direction.
On Target — you arrive exactly where intended.

Name: Teleportation Circle
Components: V, M (rare inks worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Cartographer (EFA) Artificer, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger, Horizon Walker (XGE) Ranger
Text:
As you cast the spell, you draw a 5-foot-radius circle inscribed with sigils linking your location to a permanent teleportation circle whose sigil sequence you know on the same plane. A shimmering portal opens and remains until the end of your next turn. Any creature entering the portal appears within 5 feet of the destination circle or the nearest unoccupied space.

Major temples, guildhalls, and similar places often maintain permanent circles. Each has a unique sigil sequence.

When you first gain this spell, you learn two sigil sequences on the Material Plane (DM’s choice). You may learn more by studying them for 1 minute.

Casting this spell daily in the same spot for 365 days creates a permanent teleportation circle.

Name: Floating Disk
Components: V, S, M (a drop of mercury)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
This spell creates a circular horizontal disk of force, 3 feet in diameter and 1 inch thick, floating 3 feet above the ground in an unoccupied space you can see. The disk lasts for the duration and can hold up to 500 pounds. Exceeding this weight ends the spell and drops everything on it.

The disk remains stationary while you are within 20 feet. If you move farther than 20 feet, it follows you to remain within that distance. It can traverse uneven terrain and stairs but cannot cross elevation changes of 10 feet or more.

If you move more than 100 feet from the disk (usually due to obstacles), the spell ends.

Name: Thaumaturgy
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Drakewarden (FTD) Ranger, Drakewarden (FTD) Ranger, Giant (BGG) Barbarian, Giant (BGG) Barbarian
Text:
You manifest a minor wonder within range, choosing one effect. If cast multiple times, you may have up to three 1-minute effects active.

Altered Eyes — your eyes change appearance for 1 minute.
Booming Voice — your voice becomes three times louder for 1 minute; you have Advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) checks.
Fire Play — flames flicker, brighten, dim, or change color for 1 minute.
Invisible Hand — an unlocked door or window instantly opens or slams shut.
Phantom Sound — you create an instantaneous sound at a point within range.
Tremors — harmless ground tremors occur for 1 minute.

Name: Thorn Whip
Components: V, S, M (the stem of a plant with thorns)
Subclasses: Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: You create a vine-like whip of thorns that lashes toward a creature in range. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 Piercing damage, and if it is Large or smaller, you may pull it up to 10 feet closer to you.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Thunderclap
Classes: Artificer (EFA), Bard (PHB'24), Druid (PHB'24), Sorcerer (PHB'24), Warlock (PHB'24), Wizard (PHB'24)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Moon (FRHoF) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Strength (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Thunder damage. The thunderous sound can be heard up to 100 feet away.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Thunderous Smite
Subclasses: Noble Genies (FRHoF) Paladin, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Zeal (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text: Your strike resounds with thunder audible within 300 feet. The target takes an extra 2d6 Thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must make a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away and knocked Prone.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Thunderwave
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Tempest (PHB'14) Cleric, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Fathomless (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You release a wave of thunderous force. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away; on a success, it takes half damage only.

Unsecured objects entirely within the Cube are pushed 10 feet away, and a thunderous boom is audible within 300 feet.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Time Stop
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
You briefly halt time for everyone but yourself. No time passes for other creatures while you take 1d4 + 1 turns in succession, acting normally.

The spell ends early if any action you take, or any effect you create, affects a creature other than you or an object worn or carried by another creature. It also ends if you move more than 1,000 feet from where you cast the spell.

Name: Toll the Dead
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: You point at a creature you can see within range, and a single mournful bell tolls audibly within 10 feet of the target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d8 Necrotic damage. If the target is missing any of its Hit Points, it instead takes 1d12 Necrotic damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by one die when you reach levels 5 (2d8 or 2d12), 11 (3d8 or 3d12), and 17 (4d8 or 4d12).

Name: Tongues
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Knowledge (FRHoF) Cleric
Text: This spell grants a touched creature the ability to understand any spoken or signed language it hears or sees. Additionally, when the target speaks or signs, any creature that knows at least one language can understand it, provided it can hear the speech or see the signing.

Name: Transport via Plants
Text: You create a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant of any distance on the same plane. You must have seen or touched the destination plant at least once. For the duration, any creature may step into the target plant and exit the destination plant using 5 feet of movement.

Name: Tree Stride
Subclasses: Land (Forest) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Temperate Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Ancients (PHB'14) Paladin, Ancients (PHB'24) Paladin
Text:
You gain the ability to enter a tree and travel to another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. Both trees must be living and at least your size. Entering a tree costs 5 feet of movement. Upon entering, you instantly sense all trees of the same kind within 500 feet and may pass into one of them or step out of the tree you entered, appearing within 5 feet of it and spending another 5 feet of movement.

You may use this transportation only once per turn, and you must end each turn outside a tree.

Name: True Polymorph
Components: V, S, M (a drop of mercury, a dollop of gum arabic, and a wisp of smoke)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric
Text:
Choose a creature or nonmagical object you can see within range. A creature transforms into another creature or a nonmagical object, or an object transforms into a creature (if not worn or carried). The transformation lasts for the duration or until the target dies or is destroyed. If you maintain Concentration for the full duration, the effect becomes permanent until dispelled.

An unwilling creature may make a Wisdom saving throw to resist.

Creature → Creature. The new form may be any creature with a Challenge Rating equal to or lower than the target’s CR or level. The target’s statistics are replaced by the new form’s stat block, but it retains its Hit Points, Hit Dice, alignment, and personality.

It gains Temporary Hit Points equal to the new form’s Hit Points. These vanish when the spell ends. The target is limited by the anatomy of the new form and cannot speak or cast spells. Its gear melds into the form.

Object → Creature. You may turn an object into a creature of size no larger than the object and CR 9 or lower. The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, acts immediately after you in combat, and obeys your commands. After 1 hour, you lose control, though it may remain Friendly.

Creature → Object. The creature and its gear transform into an object of size no larger than the creature. It has the statistics of the object and remembers nothing of the time spent transformed.

Name: True Resurrection
Components: V, S, M (diamonds worth 25,000+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You touch a creature dead no longer than 200 years that did not die of old age. The creature returns to life with all its Hit Points.

The spell closes all wounds, neutralizes poison, cures magical diseases, and removes curses affecting the creature at death. It restores missing organs and limbs. If the creature was Undead, it is restored to its living form.

If the original body no longer exists, you may speak the creature’s name to create a new body, which appears in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you.

Name: True Seeing
Components: V, S, M (mushroom powder worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: For the duration, a willing creature you touch gains Truesight out to 120 feet.

Name: True Strike
Components: S, M (a weapon with which you have proficiency and that is worth 1+ CP)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Diviner (PHB'24) Wizard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: Guided by magical insight, you make one attack with the weapon used to cast the spell. You use your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls. If the attack deals damage, it may be Radiant damage or the weapon’s normal type (your choice).
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. Whether you deal Radiant damage or the weapon's normal damage type, the attack deals extra Radiant damage when you reach levels 5 (1d6), 11 (2d6), and 17 (3d6).

Name: Tsunami
Text:
A massive wall of water erupts at a point you choose within range. You may make the wall up to 300 feet long, 300 feet high, and 50 feet thick. It lasts for the duration.

When it appears, each creature in its area makes a Strength saving throw, taking 6d10 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half on a success.

At the start of each of your turns, the wall and any creatures within it move 50 feet away from you. Any Huge or smaller creature inside the wall or whose space it enters must make a Strength saving throw or take 5d10 Bludgeoning damage (once per round). At the end of each turn, the wall’s height decreases by 50 feet and its damage decreases by 1d10. When its height reaches 0, the spell ends.

Creatures may swim within the wall but must succeed on a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC to move. A creature that exits the wall falls to the ground.

Name: Unseen Servant
Components: V, S, M (a bit of string and of wood)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text:
This spell creates an Invisible, mindless, shapeless Medium force that performs simple tasks. It appears in an unoccupied space on the ground within range. It has AC 10, 1 Hit Point, Strength 2, and cannot attack. If reduced to 0 Hit Points, the spell ends.

Once per turn as a Bonus Action, you may command the servant to move up to 15 feet and interact with an object. It can perform simple tasks such as fetching, cleaning, mending, folding, lighting fires, serving food, or pouring drinks. It continues the task until finished, then waits for your next command.

If commanded to move more than 60 feet from you, the spell ends.

Name: Vampiric Touch
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Death (DMG'14) Cleric, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Grave (XGE) Cleric, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Lunar (DSotDQ) Sorcerer, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Ambition (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
Your shadow-wreathed touch drains life from a creature within reach. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 3d6 Necrotic damage, and you regain Hit Points equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt.

Until the spell ends, you may repeat the attack each turn as a Magic action, targeting the same creature or a different one.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 3.

Name: Vicious Mockery
Text: You hurl a string of magically charged insults at a creature you can see or hear within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw or take 1d6 Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the end of its next turn.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Vitriolic Sphere
Components: V, S, M (a drop of bile)
Subclasses: Alchemist (EFA) Artificer, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text: You point to a location within range, launching a glowing 1-foot sphere of acid that explodes in a 20-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 10d4 Acid damage and 5d4 more at the end of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half the initial damage only.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The initial damage increases by 2d4 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Wall of Fire
Components: V, S, M (a piece of charcoal)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Celestial (PHB'24) Warlock, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Fiend (PHB'24) Warlock, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Forge (XGE) Cleric, Light (PHB'14) Cleric, Light (PHB'24) Cleric, Spellfire (FRHoF) Sorcerer, Celestial (XGE) Warlock, Fiend (PHB'14) Warlock
Text:
You create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. The wall may be up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ring up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. It is opaque and lasts for the duration.

When it appears, creatures in its area make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 5d8 Fire damage on a failed save or half on a success.

One side of the wall, chosen when you cast the spell, deals 5d8 Fire damage to creatures that end their turn within 10 feet of that side or inside the wall. A creature takes the same damage when it enters the wall or ends its turn there. The opposite side deals no damage.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 4.

Name: Wall of Force
Components: V, S, M (a shard of glass)
Subclasses: Armorer (TCE) Artificer, Armorer (EFA) Artificer, Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Clockwork (PHB'24) Sorcerer, Clockwork Soul (TCE) Sorcerer, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Redemption (XGE) Paladin, Redemption (XGE) Paladin
Text:
An Invisible wall of force appears at a point you choose within range. You may orient it horizontally, vertically, or at an angle. It may float freely or rest on a surface. You may form a hemispherical dome or globe up to a 10-foot radius, or a flat surface of ten contiguous 10-foot-square panels. The wall is 1/4 inch thick and lasts for the duration.

If the wall appears in a creature’s space, you choose which side the creature is pushed to.

Nothing can physically pass through the wall. It is immune to all damage and cannot be dispelled by Dispel Magic. A Disintegrate spell destroys it instantly. The wall extends into the Ethereal Plane and blocks ethereal travel.

Name: Wall of Ice
Components: V, S, M (a piece of quartz)
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. You may form a hemispherical dome or globe up to a 10-foot radius, or a flat surface of ten contiguous 10-foot-square panels. The wall is 1 foot thick and lasts for the duration.

If the wall appears in a creature’s space, you choose which side it is pushed to, and it makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6 Cold damage on a failed save or half on a success.

The wall is an object with AC 12 and 30 Hit Points per 10-foot section. It has Immunity to Cold, Poison, and Psychic damage and Vulnerability to Fire. Reducing a section to 0 Hit Points destroys it and leaves behind a sheet of frigid air.

A creature passing through the frigid air for the first time on a turn makes a Constitution saving throw, taking 5d6 Cold damage on a failed save or half on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage the wall deals when it appears increases by 2d6 and the damage from passing through the sheet of frigid air increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Wall of Stone
Components: V, S, M (a cube of granite)
Subclasses: Land (Desert) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Mountain) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Arid Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Dao) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You conjure a nonmagical wall of solid stone at a point within range. The wall is 6 inches thick and consists of ten contiguous 10-foot-square panels, or you may create 10-by-20-foot panels that are 3 inches thick.

If the wall appears in a creature’s space, you choose which side it is pushed to. If a creature would be fully enclosed, it may make a Dexterity saving throw to use its Reaction to move up to its Speed to avoid being trapped.

The wall may take any shape, cannot overlap creatures or objects, and must merge with existing stone for support. You may bridge gaps or create ramps.

Spans over 20 feet require halving panel size to create supports. You may crudely shape battlements.

Each panel has AC 15 and 30 Hit Points per inch of thickness and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. Destroying a panel may collapse adjacent panels.

If you maintain Concentration for the full duration, the wall becomes permanent.

Name: Wall of Thorns
Components: V, S, M (a handful of thorns)
Text:
You create a wall of thorny brush on a solid surface within range. You may make it up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick, or a 20-foot-diameter circle up to 20 feet high and 5 feet thick. It blocks line of sight.

When it appears, creatures in its area make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 Piercing damage on a failed save or half on a success.

Creatures may move through the wall at great cost: each foot of movement requires 4 feet of speed. The first time a creature enters a space in the wall on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d8 Slashing damage on a failed save or half on a success. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Both types of damage increase by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 6.

Name: Warding Bond
Components: V, S, M (a pair of platinum rings worth 50+ GP each, which you and the target must wear for the duration)
Subclasses: Battle Smith (TCE) Artificer, Battle Smith (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Peace (TCE) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric, Solidarity (PSA) (PSA) Cleric
Text:
You touch a willing creature and form a mystic bond. While the target remains within 60 feet of you, it gains a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws and has Resistance to all damage. Whenever it takes damage, you take the same amount.

The spell ends if you drop to 0 Hit Points, if you and the target are separated by more than 60 feet, or if the spell is cast again on either creature.

Name: Water Breathing
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Sea (PHB'24) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter
Text: Up to ten willing creatures within range gain the ability to breathe underwater for the duration. They retain their normal breathing methods as well.

Name: Water Walk
Components: V, S, M (a piece of cork)
Subclasses: Land (Coast) (PHB'14) Druid, Land (Swamp) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
This spell allows up to ten willing creatures you choose within range to move across any liquid surface—water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava—as though it were harmless solid ground (though molten lava can still deal heat damage).

A target must use a Bonus Action to move from the liquid’s surface into the liquid and vice versa. If a target falls into the liquid, it passes through the surface normally.

Name: Web
Components: V, S, M (a bit of spiderweb)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Land (Tropical Land) (PHB'24) Druid, Land (Underdark) (PHB'14) Druid, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger, Swarmkeeper (TCE) Ranger
Text:
You conjure sticky webs at a point within range, filling a 20-foot Cube for the duration. The webs create Difficult Terrain and lightly obscure the area.

If the webs are not anchored between solid surfaces or spread across a floor, wall, or ceiling, they collapse and the spell ends at the start of your next turn. Webs over a flat surface are 5 feet deep.

The first time a creature enters the webs on a turn or starts its turn there, it must make a Dexterity saving throw or become Restrained until it escapes.

A Restrained creature may use an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the condition on a success.

The webs are flammable. Any 5-foot Cube exposed to fire burns away in 1 round, dealing 2d4 Fire damage to creatures starting their turn in the flames.

Name: Weird
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Illusionist (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
You attempt to fill minds with illusory horrors. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 10d10 Psychic damage and becomes Frightened for the duration. On a successful save, it takes half damage only.

A Frightened creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. On a failed save, it takes 5d10 Psychic damage. On a successful save, the effect ends for that creature.

Name: Wind Walk
Text:
You and up to ten willing creatures you choose assume gaseous form, appearing as wisps of cloud. In this form, a target has a Fly Speed of 300 feet, can hover, has Immunity to the Prone condition, and has Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

The only actions a target can take are Dash or a Magic action to begin reverting to normal form. Reverting takes 1 minute, during which the target is Stunned. A target may return to cloud form with another Magic action followed by a 1-minute transformation.

If a target is flying in cloud form when the spell ends, it descends 60 feet per round for 1 minute until landing safely. If unable to land, it falls the remaining distance.

Name: Wind Wall
Components: V, S, M (a fan and a feather)
Subclasses: Artillerist (TCE) Artificer, Artillerist (EFA) Artificer, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Nature (PHB'14) Cleric, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock, Genie (Djinni) (TCE) Warlock
Text:
You create a wall of powerful wind rising from a point within range. The wall may be up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick, shaped in any continuous path along the ground. It lasts for the duration.

When it appears, creatures in its area make a Strength saving throw, taking 4d8 Bludgeoning damage on a failed save or half on a success.

The wind blocks fog, smoke, and gases. Small or smaller flying creatures and objects cannot pass through. Lightweight materials are blown upward. Ordinary arrows, bolts, and similar projectiles aimed through the wall are deflected upward and automatically miss. Large projectiles, such as boulders, are unaffected. Creatures in gaseous form cannot pass through.

Name: Wish
Subclasses: Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Arcana (SCAG) Cleric, Genie (TCE) Warlock, Genie (TCE) Warlock
Text:
Wish is the most powerful spell a mortal can cast, allowing you to reshape reality with a spoken command.

The simplest use is to duplicate any spell of level 8 or lower without needing components or requirements.

Alternatively, you may choose one of the following effects:

Object Creation — Create a nonmagical object worth up to 25,000 GP, no larger than 300 feet in any dimension, appearing in an unoccupied space you can see.

Instant Health — You and up to twenty creatures you can see regain all Hit Points and end all effects listed in Greater Restoration.

Resistance — Up to ten creatures you can see gain permanent Resistance to one damage type.

Spell Immunity — Up to ten creatures you see gain immunity to a single spell or magical effect for 8 hours.

Sudden Learning — Replace one of your feats with another for which you qualify. You lose all benefits of the old feat. You cannot replace a prerequisite feat.

Roll Redo — Force a reroll of any die rolled within the last round. You may impose Advantage or Disadvantage and choose which result is used. Reality adjusts to match the new outcome.

Reshape Reality — You may wish for something beyond the listed effects. The DM determines the result, which may be twisted, partial, or dangerous. Powerful or world-altering wishes may attract divine or cosmic consequences. Wishes affecting gods, Sigil, or the Lady of Pain automatically fail.

Casting Wish for any purpose other than duplicating a spell causes severe strain. Until you finish a Long Rest, each spell you cast deals 1d10 Necrotic damage per spell level to you, which cannot be prevented. Your Strength becomes 3 for 2d4 days. Resting reduces the remaining duration by 2 days per day of rest. There is also a 33% chance you can never cast Wish again.

Name: Witch Bolt
Components: V, S, M (a twig struck by lightning)
Subclasses: Arcane Trickster (PHB'24) Rogue, Arcane Trickster (PHB'14) Rogue, Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Eldritch Knight (PHB'24) Fighter, Eldritch Knight (PHB'14) Fighter, Evoker (PHB'24) Wizard
Text:
A beam of crackling energy shoots toward a creature within range, forming a sustained arc of lightning between you and it. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 Lightning damage.

On each of your subsequent turns, you may use a Bonus Action to deal 1d12 Lightning damage to the target automatically, even if the initial attack missed.

The spell ends if the target leaves the spell’s range or gains Total Cover from you.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The initial damage increases by 1d12 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Word of Radiance
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text: Radiant energy bursts from you in a 5-foot Emanation. Each creature of your choice that you can see within it must make a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 Radiant damage.
At Higher Levels: Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

Name: Word of Recall
Subclasses: Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer
Text:
You and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a sanctuary you previously designated. You and any creatures teleporting with you appear in the nearest unoccupied space to the location you marked when preparing your sanctuary.

If you cast this spell without having prepared a sanctuary, it has no effect.

You prepare a sanctuary by casting this spell at the chosen location.

Name: Wrathful Smite
Subclasses: Hexblade (XGE) Warlock, Hexblade (XGE) Warlock
Text: The target takes an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage from the attack and must make a Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened until the spell ends. At the end of each of its turns, the Frightened target repeats the save, ending the effect on a success.
At Higher Levels: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

Name: Regal Presence
Subclasses: Glory (PHB'24) Paladin
Text:
You surround yourself with supernatural majesty in a 10-foot Emanation. Whenever the Emanation enters the space of a creature you can see, or whenever a creature you can see enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, you may force it to make a Wisdom saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature takes 4d6 Psychic damage, falls Prone, and you may push it up to 10 feet away. On a successful save, it takes half damage only. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

Name: Zone of Truth
Subclasses: Lore (PHB'24) Bard, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Divine Soul (XGE) Sorcerer, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Monster Slayer (XGE) Ranger, Devotion (PHB'24) Paladin, Devotion (PHB'14) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Crown (SCAG) Paladin, Order (TCE) Cleric, Order (TCE) Cleric
Text:
You create a magical zone that hinders deception in a 15-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range. A creature entering the area for the first time on a turn or starting its turn there must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it cannot speak a deliberate lie while within the area. You know whether each creature succeeds or fails.

An affected creature is aware of the spell and may avoid answering questions it would normally lie about. It may be evasive, but must speak truthfully.
